built with pdoc

portfolio.services

Service layer for the portfolio app.

This module implements business logic used by the REST API and admin tasks for portfolios: rendering public/private payloads, managing portfolio aggregate operations (create/update/delete), and child services for sections, blocks, elements, projects, skills and experiences. Helper utilities for serializing values and applying element filters are included.

Docstrings follow the Google style.

   1"""Service layer for the ``portfolio`` app.
   2
   3This module implements business logic used by the REST API and admin tasks
   4for portfolios: rendering public/private payloads, managing portfolio
   5aggregate operations (create/update/delete), and child services for sections,
   6blocks, elements, projects, skills and experiences. Helper utilities for
   7serializing values and applying element filters are included.
   8
   9Docstrings follow the Google style.
  10"""
  11
  12from __future__ import annotations
  13
  14import logging
  15
  16from datetime import date, datetime, time
  17from typing import Any, Dict, Iterable, List, Sequence
  18
  19from django.core.exceptions import FieldError, ValidationError as DjangoValidationError
  20from django.db import IntegrityError, transaction
  21from django.db.models import Prefetch, QuerySet
  22from django.db.models.fields.files import FieldFile
  23
  24from django.contrib.auth import authenticate, get_user_model
  25from django.contrib.auth.password_validation import validate_password
  26from django.utils.text import slugify
  27
  28from rest_framework.exceptions import AuthenticationFailed, NotFound, PermissionDenied, ValidationError
  29from rest_framework_simplejwt.tokens import RefreshToken
  30
  31from .models import Block, Element, Experience, Portfolio, Project, Section, Skill, Theme
  32
  33
  34service_logger = logging.getLogger("portfolio.service")
  35
  36
  37OPERATOR_MAP = {
  38    "eq": "",
  39    "neq": "",
  40    "gt": "__gt",
  41    "gte": "__gte",
  42    "lt": "__lt",
  43    "lte": "__lte",
  44    "contains": "__icontains",
  45    "icontains": "__icontains",
  46    "in": "__in",
  47}
  48
  49
  50def _safe_config(config: Any) -> Dict[str, Any]:
  51    """Return a dict when ``config`` is a mapping, otherwise an empty dict.
  52
  53    Args:
  54        config: Configuration value which may be a dict or other type.
  55
  56    Returns:
  57        dict: The provided config if it is a dict, otherwise an empty dict.
  58    """
  59    if isinstance(config, dict):
  60        return config
  61    return {}
  62
  63
  64def _serialize_value(value: Any) -> Any:
  65    """Serialize model field values to JSON-friendly representations.
  66
  67    Handles common Django field types and native Python types:
  68    - ``FieldFile``: returns URL when available, otherwise string representation.
  69    - ``datetime``, ``date``, ``time``: converted to ISO 8601 strings.
  70    - basic scalar and container types are returned unchanged.
  71    - all other objects are converted with ``str()``.
  72
  73    Args:
  74        value: The value to serialize.
  75
  76    Returns:
  77        A JSON-serializable representation of ``value``.
  78    """
  79    if isinstance(value, FieldFile):
  80        if not value:
  81            return None
  82        try:
  83            return value.url
  84        except ValueError:
  85            return str(value)
  86
  87    if isinstance(value, (datetime, date, time)):
  88        return value.isoformat()
  89
  90    if value is None or isinstance(value, (str, int, float, bool, list, dict)):
  91        return value
  92
  93    return str(value)
  94
  95
  96def _get_queryset(portfolio: Portfolio, data_source: str, *, include_unpublished: bool = False) -> QuerySet:
  97    """Return a QuerySet for the requested ``data_source`` scoped to ``portfolio``.
  98
  99    Args:
 100        portfolio: Portfolio instance used to scope queries.
 101        data_source: One of the values in :class:`Element.DataSource`.
 102        include_unpublished: If True, include unpublished portfolio records.
 103
 104    Returns:
 105        Django QuerySet for the requested source; empty QuerySet on unknown source.
 106    """
 107    if data_source == Element.DataSource.PROJECT:
 108        return Project.objects.filter(portfolio=portfolio, is_visible=True)
 109
 110    if data_source == Element.DataSource.SKILL:
 111        return Skill.objects.filter(portfolio=portfolio, is_visible=True)
 112
 113    if data_source == Element.DataSource.EXPERIENCE:
 114        return Experience.objects.filter(portfolio=portfolio, is_visible=True)
 115
 116    if data_source == Element.DataSource.PORTFOLIO:
 117        qs = Portfolio.objects.filter(id=portfolio.id)
 118        if not include_unpublished:
 119            qs = qs.filter(is_published=True)
 120        return qs
 121
 122    return Portfolio.objects.none()
 123
 124
 125def _normalize_filters(filters: Any) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
 126    """Normalize a filters value into a list of dict filter descriptors.
 127
 128    If ``filters`` is not a list, or contains non-dict entries, those entries
 129    are discarded.
 130
 131    Args:
 132        filters: Raw filters value from element config.
 133
 134    Returns:
 135        A list of dicts suitable for :func:`_apply_filters`.
 136    """
 137    if not isinstance(filters, list):
 138        return []
 139    return [f for f in filters if isinstance(f, dict)]
 140
 141
 142def _apply_filters(queryset: QuerySet, filters: Sequence[Dict[str, Any]]) -> QuerySet:
 143    """Apply a sequence of filter descriptors to a QuerySet.
 144
 145    Each filter descriptor is a dict with keys: ``key``, ``operator``, and
 146    ``value``. Supported operators are mapped in :data:`OPERATOR_MAP`.
 147
 148    Args:
 149        queryset: The Django QuerySet to filter.
 150        filters: Sequence of filter descriptor dicts.
 151
 152    Returns:
 153        The filtered QuerySet.
 154    """
 155    for filter_item in filters:
 156        key = filter_item.get("key")
 157        operator = str(filter_item.get("operator", "eq")).lower()
 158        value = filter_item.get("value")
 159
 160        if not key:
 161            continue
 162
 163        lookup_suffix = OPERATOR_MAP.get(operator)
 164        if lookup_suffix is None:
 165            continue
 166
 167        if operator == "neq":
 168            try:
 169                queryset = queryset.exclude(**{key: value})
 170            except FieldError:
 171                continue
 172            continue
 173
 174        if operator == "in" and not isinstance(value, (list, tuple, set)):
 175            value = [value]
 176
 177        lookup = f"{key}{lookup_suffix}"
 178
 179        try:
 180            queryset = queryset.filter(**{lookup: value})
 181        except FieldError:
 182            continue
 183
 184    return queryset
 185
 186
 187def _filters_cache_key(filters: Sequence[Dict[str, Any]]) -> tuple:
 188    """Create a hashable cache key tuple from a sequence of filter dicts.
 189
 190    This function normalizes list/dict values into tuples so the resulting
 191    structure is hashable and can be used as a cache key.
 192
 193    Args:
 194        filters: Sequence of filter dicts.
 195
 196    Returns:
 197        A tuple suitable for use as a cache key.
 198    """
 199    normalized: List[tuple] = []
 200    for item in filters:
 201        if not isinstance(item, dict):
 202            continue
 203        key = item.get("key")
 204        operator = item.get("operator")
 205        value = item.get("value")
 206
 207        if isinstance(value, list):
 208            value_key = tuple(value)
 209        elif isinstance(value, dict):
 210            value_key = tuple(sorted(value.items()))
 211        else:
 212            value_key = value
 213
 214        normalized.append((key, operator, value_key))
 215    return tuple(normalized)
 216
 217
 218def _resolve_block_items(
 219    portfolio: Portfolio,
 220    elements: Iterable[Element],
 221    *,
 222    include_unpublished: bool = False,
 223) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
 224    """Resolve rows of data for a block from its elements.
 225
 226    For each element, the corresponding records are fetched from the
 227    appropriate model (project, skill, experience, or portfolio), element
 228    filters are applied, and the results are assembled into row dictionaries
 229    keyed by element label. Rows are aligned by record index; if sources have
 230    different lengths the shorter ones will produce ``None`` values for the
 231    missing rows.
 232
 233    Args:
 234        portfolio: Portfolio used for scoping element queries.
 235        elements: Iterable of :class:`Element` instances for the block.
 236        include_unpublished: If True, include unpublished portfolio records.
 237
 238    Returns:
 239        A list of dictionaries representing rows for the block.
 240    """
 241    element_data: List[tuple[Element, List[Any]]] = []
 242    max_rows = 0
 243
 244    record_cache: Dict[tuple, List[Any]] = {}
 245
 246    for element in elements:
 247        config = _safe_config(element.config)
 248        filters = _normalize_filters(config.get("filters", []))
 249        cache_key = (element.data_source, _filters_cache_key(filters))
 250
 251        records = record_cache.get(cache_key)
 252        if records is None:
 253            queryset = _get_queryset(portfolio, element.data_source, include_unpublished=include_unpublished)
 254            queryset = _apply_filters(queryset, filters)
 255            records = list(queryset)
 256            record_cache[cache_key] = records
 257
 258        max_rows = max(max_rows, len(records))
 259        element_data.append((element, records))
 260
 261    if max_rows == 0:
 262        return []
 263
 264    rows: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
 265    for row_index in range(max_rows):
 266        row: Dict[str, Any] = {}
 267        for element, records in element_data:
 268            record = records[row_index] if row_index < len(records) else None
 269            value = getattr(record, element.field, None) if record is not None else None
 270            row[element.label] = _serialize_value(value)
 271        rows.append(row)
 272
 273    return rows
 274
 275
 276def _fetch_blocks(section: Section, *, include_unpublished: bool = False) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
 277    """Fetch and render visible blocks for a section.
 278
 279    The rendered representation contains the block type, its safe config and
 280    the resolved items for each block.
 281
 282    Args:
 283        section: Section instance to fetch blocks for.
 284        include_unpublished: If True, include unpublished portfolio records
 285            when resolving element data.
 286
 287    Returns:
 288        A list of rendered block dictionaries.
 289    """
 290    visible_elements = Prefetch(
 291        "elements",
 292        queryset=Element.objects.filter(is_visible=True).order_by("order", "id"),
 293        to_attr="_visible_elements",
 294    )
 295
 296    blocks = (
 297        Block.objects.filter(section=section, is_visible=True)
 298        .prefetch_related(visible_elements)
 299        .order_by("order", "id")
 300    )
 301
 302    rendered_blocks: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
 303    for block in blocks:
 304        elements = list(getattr(block, "_visible_elements", []))
 305        items = _resolve_block_items(section.portfolio, elements, include_unpublished=include_unpublished)
 306
 307        rendered_blocks.append(
 308            {
 309                "type": block.type,
 310                "config": _safe_config(block.config),
 311                "items": items,
 312            }
 313        )
 314
 315    return rendered_blocks
 316
 317
 318def _fetch_sections(portfolio: Portfolio, *, include_unpublished: bool = False) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
 319    """Return rendered sections (with blocks) for a portfolio.
 320
 321    Args:
 322        portfolio: Portfolio instance to fetch sections for.
 323        include_unpublished: If True, include unpublished portfolio records
 324            when resolving element data.
 325
 326    Returns:
 327        A list of section dictionaries, each containing its rendered blocks.
 328    """
 329    sections = Section.objects.filter(portfolio=portfolio, is_visible=True).order_by("order", "id")
 330
 331    return [
 332        {
 333            "name": section.name,
 334            "config": _safe_config(section.config),
 335            "blocks": _fetch_blocks(section, include_unpublished=include_unpublished),
 336        }
 337        for section in sections
 338    ]
 339
 340
 341class PortfolioRenderService:
 342    """Service responsible for producing the JSON-serializable portfolio payload.
 343
 344    This class encapsulates the rendering logic for both private (authenticated)
 345    and public portfolio representations.
 346    """
 347
 348    def _serialize_payload(self, *, portfolio: Portfolio, include_unpublished: bool) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 349        """Serialize a Portfolio instance into a nested data payload.
 350
 351        Args:
 352            portfolio: The Portfolio instance to serialize.
 353            include_unpublished: Whether to include unpublished child records
 354                when resolving element data.
 355
 356        Returns:
 357            A dictionary representing the portfolio, suitable for JSON response.
 358        """
 359        theme_data = None
 360        if portfolio.theme is not None:
 361            theme_data = {
 362                "name": portfolio.theme.name,
 363                "config": _safe_config(portfolio.theme.config),
 364            }
 365
 366        return {
 367            "portfolio": {
 368                "title": portfolio.title,
 369                "slug": portfolio.slug,
 370                "description": portfolio.description,
 371                "resume": _serialize_value(portfolio.resume),
 372                "theme": theme_data,
 373                "sections": _fetch_sections(portfolio, include_unpublished=include_unpublished),
 374            }
 375        }
 376
 377    def render_portfolio(
 378        self,
 379        portfolio_id: int,
 380        user_id: int,
 381        *,
 382        include_unpublished: bool = False,
 383    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 384        """Render a portfolio owned by ``user_id``.
 385
 386        Args:
 387            portfolio_id: ID of the portfolio to render.
 388            user_id: ID of the owning user.
 389            include_unpublished: If True, include unpublished children in payload.
 390
 391        Raises:
 392            rest_framework.exceptions.NotFound: If the portfolio does not exist.
 393
 394        Returns:
 395            Serialized portfolio payload as a dict.
 396        """
 397        qs = Portfolio.objects.select_related("theme", "user").filter(id=portfolio_id, user_id=user_id)
 398        if not include_unpublished:
 399            qs = qs.filter(is_published=True)
 400
 401        portfolio = qs.first()
 402        if portfolio is None:
 403            raise NotFound("Portfolio not found")
 404
 405        return self._serialize_payload(portfolio=portfolio, include_unpublished=include_unpublished)
 406
 407    def render_public_portfolio_by_slug(self, slug: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 408        """Render a public portfolio identified by ``slug``.
 409
 410        Args:
 411            slug: Public slug string to look up.
 412
 413        Raises:
 414            rest_framework.exceptions.NotFound: If no published portfolio matches.
 415
 416        Returns:
 417            Serialized portfolio payload as a dict.
 418        """
 419        slug_value = str(slug or "").strip()
 420        if not slug_value:
 421            raise NotFound("Portfolio not found")
 422
 423        portfolio = (
 424            Portfolio.objects.select_related("theme", "user")
 425            .filter(slug=slug_value, is_published=True)
 426            .order_by("-updated_at", "-id")
 427            .first()
 428        )
 429        if portfolio is None:
 430            raise NotFound("Portfolio not found")
 431
 432        return self._serialize_payload(portfolio=portfolio, include_unpublished=False)
 433
 434
 435class PortfolioService:
 436    """Service for creating, updating and deleting portfolio aggregates.
 437
 438    This class contains validation, slug generation and publish-time checks
 439    for Portfolio entities.
 440    """
 441
 442    _ALLOWED_FIELDS = {"title", "slug", "description", "resume", "theme", "is_published"}
 443
 444    def list(self, *, user) -> QuerySet[Portfolio]:
 445        """List portfolios owned by ``user``.
 446
 447        Args:
 448            user: The user whose portfolios should be returned.
 449
 450        Returns:
 451            QuerySet of :class:`Portfolio` ordered by creation time.
 452        """
 453        return Portfolio.objects.filter(user=user).select_related("theme").order_by("-created_at", "-id")
 454
 455    def retrieve(self, *, user, portfolio_id: int) -> Portfolio:
 456        """Retrieve a portfolio by id for a given user.
 457
 458        Args:
 459            user: Owner of the portfolio.
 460            portfolio_id: ID of the portfolio to retrieve.
 461
 462        Raises:
 463            rest_framework.exceptions.NotFound: If not found.
 464
 465        Returns:
 466            The :class:`Portfolio` instance.
 467        """
 468        try:
 469            return Portfolio.objects.select_related("theme").get(id=portfolio_id, user=user)
 470        except Portfolio.DoesNotExist:
 471            raise NotFound("Portfolio not found.")
 472
 473    def _ensure_owner(self, *, user, portfolio: Portfolio) -> None:
 474        """Raise ``PermissionDenied`` when ``user`` is not the portfolio owner.
 475
 476        Args:
 477            user: The requesting user.
 478            portfolio: Portfolio instance being accessed.
 479
 480        Raises:
 481            rest_framework.exceptions.PermissionDenied: If the user is not the owner.
 482        """
 483        if portfolio.user_id != user.id:
 484            raise PermissionDenied("You do not have permission to access this portfolio.")
 485
 486    def _coerce_bool(self, value: Any, *, default: bool = False) -> bool:
 487        """Coerce common truthy/falsy representations into a boolean.
 488
 489        Accepts booleans, numeric and string values ("true", "1", "yes", etc.).
 490
 491        Args:
 492            value: Value to coerce.
 493            default: Default boolean to return when ``value`` is ``None``.
 494
 495        Returns:
 496            bool: Coerced boolean value.
 497
 498        Raises:
 499            rest_framework.exceptions.ValidationError: If the value cannot be parsed.
 500        """
 501        if value is None:
 502            return default
 503        if isinstance(value, bool):
 504            return value
 505        if isinstance(value, (int, float)):
 506            return bool(value)
 507        if isinstance(value, str):
 508            normalized = value.strip().lower()
 509            if normalized in {"true", "1", "yes", "y", "on"}:
 510                return True
 511            if normalized in {"false", "0", "no", "n", "off"}:
 512                return False
 513        raise ValidationError({"is_published": "Invalid boolean value."})
 514
 515    def _slug_max_length(self) -> int:
 516        """Return the configured max_length for the Portfolio.slug field.
 517
 518        Returns:
 519            int: Maximum length (fallback 50).
 520        """
 521        field = Portfolio._meta.get_field("slug")
 522        return int(getattr(field, "max_length", 50) or 50)
 523
 524    def _normalize_title(self, value: Any) -> str:
 525        """Normalize and validate a portfolio title.
 526
 527        Args:
 528            value: Title value to normalize.
 529
 530        Returns:
 531            The normalized title string.
 532
 533        Raises:
 534            rest_framework.exceptions.ValidationError: If the title is empty.
 535        """
 536        title = "" if value is None else str(value)
 537        title = title.strip()
 538        if not title:
 539            raise ValidationError({"title": "Title cannot be empty."})
 540        return title
 541
 542    def _normalize_slug_source(self, value: Any) -> str:
 543        """Normalize a slug source string and apply ``slugify``.
 544
 545        Strips leading/trailing dashes from the slug.
 546
 547        Args:
 548            value: Raw slug source value.
 549
 550        Returns:
 551            Normalized slug string.
 552        """
 553        slug = "" if value is None else str(value)
 554        slug = slug.strip()
 555        slug = slugify(slug)
 556        return slug.strip("-")
 557
 558    def _make_unique_slug(self, *, user, base_slug: str, exclude_portfolio_id: int | None = None) -> str:
 559        """Generate a user-scoped unique slug based on ``base_slug``.
 560
 561        If the candidate slug already exists for the user, a numeric suffix is
 562        appended to make it unique while respecting the field max length.
 563
 564        Args:
 565            user: The owner user used to scope uniqueness checks.
 566            base_slug: The normalized base slug to start from.
 567            exclude_portfolio_id: Optional portfolio id to exclude from checks
 568                (useful when updating an existing portfolio).
 569
 570        Returns:
 571            A unique slug string for the user.
 572
 573        Raises:
 574            rest_framework.exceptions.ValidationError: If the base_slug is empty.
 575        """
 576        max_len = self._slug_max_length()
 577        base_slug = (base_slug or "").strip("-")[:max_len].strip("-")
 578        if not base_slug:
 579            raise ValidationError({"slug": "Slug cannot be empty."})
 580
 581        qs = Portfolio.objects.filter(user=user)
 582        if exclude_portfolio_id is not None:
 583            qs = qs.exclude(pk=exclude_portfolio_id)
 584
 585        candidate = base_slug
 586        suffix = 0
 587        while qs.filter(slug=candidate).exists():
 588            suffix += 1
 589            suffix_str = f"-{suffix}"
 590            trimmed = base_slug[: max_len - len(suffix_str)].rstrip("-")
 591            candidate = f"{trimmed}{suffix_str}"
 592
 593        return candidate
 594
 595    def _resolve_theme(self, value: Any) -> Theme | None:
 596        """Resolve a theme reference to a :class:`Theme` instance.
 597
 598        Accepts a Theme instance, an integer id, or ``None``/empty string.
 599
 600        Args:
 601            value: Theme id or :class:`Theme` instance.
 602
 603        Returns:
 604            Theme or None.
 605
 606        Raises:
 607            rest_framework.exceptions.ValidationError: On invalid references.
 608        """
 609        if value is None or value == "":
 610            return None
 611
 612        if isinstance(value, Theme):
 613            return value
 614
 615        try:
 616            theme_id = int(value)
 617        except (TypeError, ValueError):
 618            raise ValidationError({"theme": "Invalid theme reference."})
 619
 620        try:
 621            return Theme.objects.get(pk=theme_id)
 622        except Theme.DoesNotExist:
 623            raise ValidationError({"theme": "Theme not found."})
 624
 625    def _assert_publishable(self, portfolio: Portfolio) -> None:
 626        """Validate that a portfolio can be published.
 627
 628        A publishable portfolio must be persisted and contain at least one
 629        visible section and at least one block.
 630
 631        Raises:
 632            rest_framework.exceptions.ValidationError: If the portfolio is not publishable.
 633        """
 634        if portfolio.pk is None:
 635            raise ValidationError(
 636                {
 637                    "is_published": "Portfolio must be saved and have at least one section and at least one block before publishing."
 638                }
 639            )
 640
 641        has_section = Section.objects.filter(portfolio_id=portfolio.pk).exists()
 642        has_block = Block.objects.filter(section__portfolio_id=portfolio.pk).exists()
 643
 644        if not has_section or not has_block:
 645            raise ValidationError(
 646                {
 647                    "is_published": "Portfolio must have at least one section and at least one block before publishing."
 648                }
 649            )
 650
 651    def _reject_unknown_fields(self, payload: Any) -> None:
 652        """Reject unexpected keys in payloads.
 653
 654        Args:
 655            payload: A mapping-like object containing candidate fields.
 656
 657        Raises:
 658            rest_framework.exceptions.ValidationError: If unknown fields are present.
 659        """
 660        keys = set(getattr(payload, "keys", lambda: [])())
 661        unknown = keys - self._ALLOWED_FIELDS
 662        if unknown:
 663            raise ValidationError({key: "Unknown field." for key in sorted(unknown)})
 664
 665    @transaction.atomic
 666    def create(self, *, user, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Portfolio:
 667        """Create a new Portfolio aggregate.
 668
 669        Performs validation, slug normalization, and optional publish checks.
 670
 671        Args:
 672            user: Owner of the new portfolio.
 673            validated_data: Dict of validated input fields.
 674
 675        Returns:
 676            The created :class:`Portfolio` instance.
 677        """
 678        service_logger.info(
 679            "portfolio.create user_id=%s keys=%s",
 680            getattr(user, "id", None),
 681            sorted(list(validated_data.keys())),
 682        )
 683        self._reject_unknown_fields(validated_data)
 684
 685        if validated_data.get("title", None) is None:
 686            raise ValidationError({"title": "This field is required."})
 687
 688        title = self._normalize_title(validated_data.get("title"))
 689        description = validated_data.get("description", "")
 690        if description is None:
 691            description = ""
 692        description = str(description)
 693
 694        raw_slug = validated_data.get("slug")
 695        base_slug = (
 696            self._normalize_slug_source(raw_slug)
 697            if raw_slug not in (None, "")
 698            else slugify(title)
 699        )
 700        slug = self._make_unique_slug(user=user, base_slug=base_slug)
 701
 702        theme = self._resolve_theme(validated_data.get("theme", None))
 703        publish_requested = self._coerce_bool(validated_data.get("is_published", False), default=False)
 704
 705        # Create independently valid aggregate root (unpublished by default).
 706        portfolio = Portfolio(
 707            user=user,
 708            title=title,
 709            slug=slug,
 710            description=description,
 711            resume=validated_data.get("resume"),
 712            theme=theme,
 713            is_published=False,
 714        )
 715
 716        try:
 717            portfolio.full_clean(exclude=None)
 718        except DjangoValidationError as exc:
 719            raise ValidationError(exc.message_dict or {"detail": exc.messages})
 720
 721        try:
 722            portfolio.save()
 723        except IntegrityError:
 724            raise ValidationError({"detail": "Could not create portfolio."})
 725
 726        # Only enforce child existence when publishing.
 727        if publish_requested:
 728            self._assert_publishable(portfolio)
 729            portfolio.is_published = True
 730            portfolio.save(update_fields=["is_published", "updated_at"])
 731
 732        return portfolio
 733
 734    @transaction.atomic
 735    def update(self, *, user, instance: Portfolio, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Portfolio:
 736        """Update a Portfolio aggregate with validated data.
 737
 738        Args:
 739            user: Requesting user (must be owner).
 740            instance: Portfolio instance to update.
 741            validated_data: Dict of validated updates.
 742
 743        Returns:
 744            Updated :class:`Portfolio` instance.
 745        """
 746        service_logger.info(
 747            "portfolio.update user_id=%s portfolio_id=%s keys=%s",
 748            getattr(user, "id", None),
 749            getattr(instance, "id", None),
 750            sorted(list(validated_data.keys())),
 751        )
 752        self._ensure_owner(user=user, portfolio=instance)
 753
 754        payload = validated_data
 755        self._reject_unknown_fields(payload)
 756
 757        if "title" in payload:
 758            instance.title = self._normalize_title(payload.get("title"))
 759
 760        if "description" in payload:
 761            description = payload.get("description")
 762            if description is None:
 763                description = ""
 764            instance.description = str(description)
 765
 766        if "resume" in payload:
 767            instance.resume = payload.get("resume")
 768
 769        if "theme" in payload:
 770            instance.theme = self._resolve_theme(payload.get("theme"))
 771
 772        if "slug" in payload:
 773            raw_slug = payload.get("slug")
 774            if raw_slug in (None, ""):
 775                base_slug = slugify(instance.title)
 776            else:
 777                base_slug = self._normalize_slug_source(raw_slug)
 778            instance.slug = self._make_unique_slug(
 779                user=user,
 780                base_slug=base_slug,
 781                exclude_portfolio_id=instance.pk,
 782            )
 783
 784        publish_requested = "is_published" in payload
 785        if publish_requested:
 786            new_is_published = self._coerce_bool(payload.get("is_published"), default=instance.is_published)
 787            if new_is_published and not instance.is_published:
 788                self._assert_publishable(instance)
 789            instance.is_published = new_is_published
 790
 791        try:
 792            instance.full_clean(exclude=None)
 793            instance.save()
 794        except DjangoValidationError as exc:
 795            raise ValidationError(exc.message_dict or {"detail": exc.messages})
 796        except IntegrityError:
 797            raise ValidationError({"detail": "Could not update portfolio."})
 798
 799        return instance
 800
 801    @transaction.atomic
 802    def delete(self, *, user, instance: Portfolio) -> None:
 803        """Delete a portfolio after ensuring the requester is the owner.
 804
 805        Args:
 806            user: Requesting user.
 807            instance: Portfolio to delete.
 808        """
 809        service_logger.info(
 810            "portfolio.delete user_id=%s portfolio_id=%s",
 811            getattr(user, "id", None),
 812            getattr(instance, "id", None),
 813        )
 814        self._ensure_owner(user=user, portfolio=instance)
 815        instance.delete()
 816
 817
 818class BasePortfolioChildService:
 819    """Base helper for services that manage portfolio-scoped child models.
 820
 821    Provides common ordering, creation and update patterns used by
 822    :class:`ProjectService`, :class:`SkillService`, :class:`ExperienceService`,
 823    :class:`SectionService`, :class:`BlockService` and :class:`ElementService`.
 824    """
 825
 826    model = None
 827    allowed_fields: set[str] = set()
 828    not_found_message = "Object not found."
 829    create_error_message = "Could not create object."
 830    update_error_message = "Could not update object."
 831    ownership_error_message = "Object does not belong to this portfolio."
 832
 833    def _resolve_portfolio(self, *, portfolio_id: int, user, for_update: bool = False) -> Portfolio:
 834        """Resolve a portfolio for owner-scoped operations.
 835
 836        Args:
 837            portfolio_id: Portfolio id to resolve.
 838            user: Requesting user.
 839            for_update: If True, use ``select_for_update`` to lock rows.
 840
 841        Raises:
 842            rest_framework.exceptions.NotFound: If portfolio is not found.
 843
 844        Returns:
 845            The resolved :class:`Portfolio` instance.
 846        """
 847        qs = Portfolio.objects
 848        if for_update:
 849            qs = qs.select_for_update()
 850
 851        portfolio = qs.filter(id=portfolio_id, user_id=user.id).first()
 852        if portfolio is None:
 853            raise NotFound("Portfolio not found.")
 854
 855        return portfolio
 856
 857    def _resolve_instance(self, *, portfolio_id: int, object_id: int, user, for_update: bool = False):
 858        """Resolve a child instance belonging to a portfolio.
 859
 860        Args:
 861            portfolio_id: Owning portfolio id.
 862            object_id: The child's id.
 863            user: Requesting user.
 864            for_update: Use ``select_for_update`` when True.
 865
 866        Raises:
 867            rest_framework.exceptions.NotFound: If the object does not exist.
 868
 869        Returns:
 870            The model instance.
 871        """
 872        qs = self.model.objects.select_related("portfolio")
 873        if for_update:
 874            qs = qs.select_for_update()
 875
 876        try:
 877            return qs.get(id=object_id, portfolio_id=portfolio_id, portfolio__user_id=user.id)
 878        except self.model.DoesNotExist:
 879            raise NotFound(self.not_found_message)
 880
 881    def _reject_unknown_fields(self, validated_data: Any) -> None:
 882        """Reject unknown payload keys based on ``allowed_fields``.
 883
 884        Args:
 885            validated_data: Mapping-like object of payload fields.
 886
 887        Raises:
 888            rest_framework.exceptions.ValidationError: If unknown keys exist.
 889        """
 890        keys = set(getattr(validated_data, "keys", lambda: [])())
 891        unknown = keys - self.allowed_fields
 892        if unknown:
 893            raise ValidationError({key: "Unknown field." for key in sorted(unknown)})
 894
 895    def _get_queryset(self, portfolio: Portfolio) -> QuerySet:
 896        """Return a QuerySet for this service's ``model`` scoped to ``portfolio``."""
 897        return self.model.objects.filter(portfolio=portfolio)
 898
 899    def _coerce_order(self, value: Any) -> int:
 900        """Coerce and validate an order value to a positive integer.
 901
 902        Raises ValidationError for invalid or missing values.
 903        """
 904        if value is None:
 905            raise ValidationError({"order": "Order cannot be null."})
 906
 907        try:
 908            order = int(value)
 909        except (TypeError, ValueError):
 910            raise ValidationError({"order": "Order must be an integer."})
 911
 912        return max(1, order)
 913
 914    def _lock_siblings(self, *, portfolio: Portfolio) -> List[Any]:
 915        """Lock and return sibling instances ordered by order,id for mutation."""
 916        return list(
 917            self._get_queryset(portfolio)
 918            .select_for_update()
 919            .order_by("order", "id")
 920        )
 921
 922    def _resequence_orders(self, *, siblings: List[Any]) -> None:
 923        """Normalize ordering to a contiguous 1..n sequence.
 924
 925        Bulk-updates any changed order values.
 926        """
 927        changed: List[Any] = []
 928        expected = 1
 929        for instance in siblings:
 930            if int(getattr(instance, "order", 0)) != expected:
 931                instance.order = expected
 932                changed.append(instance)
 933            expected += 1
 934
 935        if changed:
 936            self.model.objects.bulk_update(changed, ["order"])
 937
 938    def _with_auto_order(self, *, portfolio: Portfolio, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 939        """Return a payload that fills an ``order`` if missing based on siblings."""
 940        payload = dict(validated_data)
 941        if "order" not in payload or payload.get("order") is None:
 942            last_order = (
 943                self._get_queryset(portfolio)
 944                .order_by("-order", "-id")
 945                .values_list("order", flat=True)
 946                .first()
 947            )
 948            payload["order"] = (last_order + 1) if last_order is not None else 1
 949        return payload
 950
 951    def list(self, *, portfolio_id: int, user) -> QuerySet:
 952        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, user=user)
 953        return self._get_queryset(portfolio).order_by("order", "id")
 954
 955    def retrieve(self, *, portfolio_id: int, object_id: int, user):
 956        return self._resolve_instance(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, object_id=object_id, user=user)
 957
 958    @transaction.atomic
 959    def create(self, *, portfolio_id: int, user, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]):
 960        self._reject_unknown_fields(validated_data)
 961        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, user=user)
 962        payload = dict(validated_data)
 963
 964        siblings = self._lock_siblings(portfolio=portfolio)
 965        self._resequence_orders(siblings=siblings)
 966
 967        desired_order = payload.get("order")
 968        if desired_order is None:
 969            desired_order = (siblings[-1].order + 1) if siblings else 1
 970        else:
 971            desired_order = self._coerce_order(desired_order)
 972
 973        max_allowed = len(siblings) + 1
 974        desired_order = max(1, min(int(desired_order), max_allowed))
 975        payload["order"] = desired_order
 976
 977        shifted: List[Any] = []
 978        for sibling in siblings:
 979            if int(getattr(sibling, "order", 0)) >= desired_order:
 980                sibling.order = int(getattr(sibling, "order", 0)) + 1
 981                shifted.append(sibling)
 982
 983        if shifted:
 984            self.model.objects.bulk_update(shifted, ["order"])
 985
 986        instance = self.model(portfolio=portfolio, **payload)
 987
 988        try:
 989            instance.full_clean(exclude=None)
 990        except DjangoValidationError as exc:
 991            raise ValidationError(exc.message_dict or {"detail": exc.messages})
 992
 993        try:
 994            instance.save()
 995        except IntegrityError:
 996            raise ValidationError({"detail": self.create_error_message})
 997
 998        return instance
 999
1000    @transaction.atomic
1001    def update(self, *, instance, user, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]):
1002        self._reject_unknown_fields(validated_data)
1003        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=instance.portfolio_id, user=user)
1004
1005        siblings = self._lock_siblings(portfolio=portfolio)
1006        self._resequence_orders(siblings=siblings)
1007
1008        locked_instance = next((s for s in siblings if s.id == instance.id), None)
1009        if locked_instance is None:
1010            raise NotFound(self.not_found_message)
1011
1012        desired_order: int | None = None
1013        if "order" in validated_data:
1014            desired_order = self._coerce_order(validated_data.get("order"))
1015            desired_order = max(1, min(int(desired_order), len(siblings)))
1016
1017        for key, value in validated_data.items():
1018            if key == "order":
1019                continue
1020            setattr(locked_instance, key, value)
1021
1022        if desired_order is not None:
1023            current_order = int(getattr(locked_instance, "order", 1))
1024            if desired_order != current_order:
1025                affected: List[Any] = []
1026                if desired_order < current_order:
1027                    for sibling in siblings:
1028                        if sibling.id == locked_instance.id:
1029                            continue
1030                        if desired_order <= int(getattr(sibling, "order", 0)) < current_order:
1031                            sibling.order = int(getattr(sibling, "order", 0)) + 1
1032                            affected.append(sibling)
1033                else:
1034                    for sibling in siblings:
1035                        if sibling.id == locked_instance.id:
1036                            continue
1037                        if current_order < int(getattr(sibling, "order", 0)) <= desired_order:
1038                            sibling.order = int(getattr(sibling, "order", 0)) - 1
1039                            affected.append(sibling)
1040
1041                if affected:
1042                    self.model.objects.bulk_update(affected, ["order"])
1043                locked_instance.order = desired_order
1044
1045        try:
1046            locked_instance.full_clean(exclude=None)
1047            locked_instance.save()
1048        except DjangoValidationError as exc:
1049            raise ValidationError(exc.message_dict or {"detail": exc.messages})
1050        except IntegrityError:
1051            raise ValidationError({"detail": self.update_error_message})
1052
1053        return locked_instance
1054
1055    @transaction.atomic
1056    def delete(self, *, instance, user) -> None:
1057        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=instance.portfolio_id, user=user)
1058
1059        siblings = self._lock_siblings(portfolio=portfolio)
1060        self._resequence_orders(siblings=siblings)
1061
1062        locked_instance = next((s for s in siblings if s.id == instance.id), None)
1063        if locked_instance is None:
1064            raise NotFound(self.not_found_message)
1065
1066        deleted_order = int(getattr(locked_instance, "order", 1))
1067        locked_instance.delete()
1068
1069        affected: List[Any] = []
1070        for sibling in siblings:
1071            if sibling.id == locked_instance.id:
1072                continue
1073            if int(getattr(sibling, "order", 0)) > deleted_order:
1074                sibling.order = int(getattr(sibling, "order", 0)) - 1
1075                affected.append(sibling)
1076
1077        if affected:
1078            self.model.objects.bulk_update(affected, ["order"])
1079
1080
1081class SectionService:
1082    """Service managing portfolio sections and their ordering."""
1083
1084    allowed_fields = {"name", "order", "is_visible", "config"}
1085    not_found_message = "Section not found."
1086    create_error_message = "Could not create section."
1087    update_error_message = "Could not update section."
1088
1089    def _normalize_name(self, value: Any) -> str:
1090        name = "" if value is None else str(value)
1091        name = name.strip()
1092        if not name:
1093            raise ValidationError({"name": "Name cannot be empty."})
1094        return name
1095
1096    def _normalize_order(self, value: Any) -> int:
1097        try:
1098            order = int(value)
1099        except (TypeError, ValueError):
1100            raise ValidationError({"order": "Order must be an integer."})
1101
1102        # Sections are typically 1-based in the seeded dataset and UI ordering.
1103        if order < 1:
1104            raise ValidationError({"order": "Order must be >= 1."})
1105        return order
1106
1107    def _normalize_config(self, value: Any) -> Dict[str, Any]:
1108        if value is None:
1109            raise ValidationError({"config": "Config must be an object."})
1110        if not isinstance(value, dict):
1111            raise ValidationError({"config": "Config must be an object."})
1112        return value
1113
1114    def _resolve_portfolio(self, *, portfolio_id: int, user, for_update: bool = False) -> Portfolio:
1115        qs = Portfolio.objects
1116        if for_update:
1117            qs = qs.select_for_update()
1118        portfolio = qs.filter(id=portfolio_id, user_id=user.id).first()
1119        if portfolio is None:
1120            raise NotFound("Portfolio not found.")
1121        return portfolio
1122
1123    def _resolve_section(self, *, portfolio_id: int, section_id: int, user, for_update: bool = False) -> Section:
1124        qs = Section.objects.select_related("portfolio")
1125        if for_update:
1126            qs = qs.select_for_update()
1127        try:
1128            return qs.get(id=section_id, portfolio_id=portfolio_id, portfolio__user_id=user.id)
1129        except Section.DoesNotExist:
1130            raise NotFound(self.not_found_message)
1131
1132    def _reject_unknown_fields(self, validated_data: Any) -> None:
1133        keys = set(getattr(validated_data, "keys", lambda: [])())
1134        unknown = keys - self.allowed_fields
1135        if unknown:
1136            raise ValidationError({key: "Unknown field." for key in sorted(unknown)})
1137
1138    def _lock_sections(self, *, portfolio: Portfolio) -> List[Section]:
1139        return list(
1140            Section.objects.select_for_update()
1141            .filter(portfolio=portfolio)
1142            .order_by("order", "id")
1143        )
1144
1145    def _resequence_orders(self, *, sections: List[Section]) -> None:
1146        """Force stable, contiguous ordering (1..n) within a portfolio."""
1147
1148        changed: List[Section] = []
1149        expected = 1
1150        for section in sections:
1151            if section.order != expected:
1152                section.order = expected
1153                changed.append(section)
1154            expected += 1
1155
1156        if changed:
1157            Section.objects.bulk_update(changed, ["order"])
1158
1159    def _maybe_unpublish_if_invalid(self, *, portfolio: Portfolio) -> None:
1160        if not portfolio.is_published:
1161            return
1162
1163        has_section = Section.objects.filter(portfolio_id=portfolio.pk).exists()
1164        has_block = Block.objects.filter(section__portfolio_id=portfolio.pk).exists()
1165
1166        if has_section and has_block:
1167            return
1168
1169        portfolio.is_published = False
1170        portfolio.save(update_fields=["is_published", "updated_at"])
1171
1172    def list(self, *, portfolio_id: int, user) -> QuerySet[Section]:
1173        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, user=user)
1174        return Section.objects.filter(portfolio=portfolio).order_by("order", "id")
1175
1176    def retrieve(self, *, portfolio_id: int, section_id: int, user) -> Section:
1177        return self._resolve_section(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, section_id=section_id, user=user)
1178
1179    @transaction.atomic
1180    def create(self, *, portfolio_id: int, user, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Section:
1181        self._reject_unknown_fields(validated_data)
1182        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, user=user)
1183
1184        payload = dict(validated_data)
1185
1186        if payload.get("name", None) is None:
1187            raise ValidationError({"name": "This field is required."})
1188
1189        payload["name"] = self._normalize_name(payload.get("name"))
1190        if "config" in payload:
1191            payload["config"] = self._normalize_config(payload.get("config"))
1192
1193        sections = self._lock_sections(portfolio=portfolio)
1194        self._resequence_orders(sections=sections)
1195
1196        desired_order = payload.get("order")
1197        if desired_order is None:
1198            desired_order = (sections[-1].order + 1) if sections else 1
1199        else:
1200            desired_order = self._normalize_order(desired_order)
1201
1202        max_allowed = len(sections) + 1
1203        desired_order = max(1, min(int(desired_order), max_allowed))
1204        payload["order"] = desired_order
1205
1206        # Insert behavior: shift existing sections down.
1207        shifted: List[Section] = []
1208        for section in sections:
1209            if section.order >= desired_order:
1210                section.order += 1
1211                shifted.append(section)
1212        if shifted:
1213            Section.objects.bulk_update(shifted, ["order"])
1214
1215        instance = Section(portfolio=portfolio, **payload)
1216
1217        try:
1218            instance.full_clean(exclude=None)
1219        except DjangoValidationError as exc:
1220            raise ValidationError(exc.message_dict or {"detail": exc.messages})
1221
1222        try:
1223            instance.save()
1224        except IntegrityError:
1225            raise ValidationError({"detail": self.create_error_message})
1226
1227        return instance
1228
1229    @transaction.atomic
1230    def update(self, *, instance: Section, user, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Section:
1231        self._reject_unknown_fields(validated_data)
1232        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=instance.portfolio_id, user=user)
1233
1234        sections = self._lock_sections(portfolio=portfolio)
1235        self._resequence_orders(sections=sections)
1236
1237        locked_instance = next((s for s in sections if s.id == instance.id), None)
1238        if locked_instance is None:
1239            raise NotFound(self.not_found_message)
1240
1241        if "name" in validated_data:
1242            locked_instance.name = self._normalize_name(validated_data.get("name"))
1243        if "config" in validated_data:
1244            locked_instance.config = self._normalize_config(validated_data.get("config"))
1245        if "is_visible" in validated_data:
1246            locked_instance.is_visible = validated_data.get("is_visible")
1247
1248        if "order" in validated_data:
1249            desired_order = self._normalize_order(validated_data.get("order"))
1250            desired_order = max(1, min(int(desired_order), len(sections)))
1251            current_order = int(locked_instance.order)
1252
1253            if desired_order != current_order:
1254                affected: List[Section] = []
1255                if desired_order < current_order:
1256                    for section in sections:
1257                        if section.id == locked_instance.id:
1258                            continue
1259                        if desired_order <= int(section.order) < current_order:
1260                            section.order = int(section.order) + 1
1261                            affected.append(section)
1262                else:
1263                    for section in sections:
1264                        if section.id == locked_instance.id:
1265                            continue
1266                        if current_order < int(section.order) <= desired_order:
1267                            section.order = int(section.order) - 1
1268                            affected.append(section)
1269
1270                if affected:
1271                    Section.objects.bulk_update(affected, ["order"])
1272                locked_instance.order = desired_order
1273
1274        try:
1275            locked_instance.full_clean(exclude=None)
1276            locked_instance.save()
1277        except DjangoValidationError as exc:
1278            raise ValidationError(exc.message_dict or {"detail": exc.messages})
1279        except IntegrityError:
1280            raise ValidationError({"detail": self.update_error_message})
1281
1282        return locked_instance
1283
1284    @transaction.atomic
1285    def delete(self, *, instance: Section, user) -> None:
1286        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=instance.portfolio_id, user=user)
1287
1288        sections = self._lock_sections(portfolio=portfolio)
1289        self._resequence_orders(sections=sections)
1290
1291        locked_instance = next((s for s in sections if s.id == instance.id), None)
1292        if locked_instance is None:
1293            raise NotFound(self.not_found_message)
1294
1295        deleted_order = int(locked_instance.order)
1296        locked_instance.delete()
1297
1298        affected: List[Section] = []
1299        for section in sections:
1300            if section.id == locked_instance.id:
1301                continue
1302            if int(section.order) > deleted_order:
1303                section.order = int(section.order) - 1
1304                affected.append(section)
1305
1306        if affected:
1307            Section.objects.bulk_update(affected, ["order"])
1308
1309        self._maybe_unpublish_if_invalid(portfolio=portfolio)
1310
1311    @transaction.atomic
1312    def reorder(self, *, portfolio_id: int, user, order_pairs: Sequence[tuple[int, int]]) -> List[Section]:
1313        """Reorder sections using (section_id, desired_order) pairs.
1314
1315        Applies a stable sort and then resequences to contiguous 1..n.
1316        """
1317
1318        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, user=user)
1319        sections = self._lock_sections(portfolio=portfolio)
1320        self._resequence_orders(sections=sections)
1321
1322        desired_map: Dict[int, int] = {}
1323        for section_id, desired_order in order_pairs:
1324            try:
1325                desired_map[int(section_id)] = self._normalize_order(desired_order)
1326            except (TypeError, ValueError):
1327                raise ValidationError({"order": "Invalid reorder payload."})
1328
1329        ids = {s.id for s in sections}
1330        unknown = sorted([sid for sid in desired_map.keys() if sid not in ids])
1331        if unknown:
1332            raise ValidationError({"detail": "One or more sections do not exist."})
1333
1334        def sort_key(section: Section) -> tuple:
1335            desired = desired_map.get(section.id)
1336            if desired is None:
1337                return (10**9, int(section.order), section.id)
1338            return (int(desired), int(section.order), section.id)
1339
1340        sections_sorted = sorted(sections, key=sort_key)
1341        expected = 1
1342        changed: List[Section] = []
1343        for section in sections_sorted:
1344            if section.order != expected:
1345                section.order = expected
1346                changed.append(section)
1347            expected += 1
1348
1349        if changed:
1350            Section.objects.bulk_update(changed, ["order"])
1351
1352        return list(Section.objects.filter(portfolio=portfolio).order_by("order", "id"))
1353
1354    @transaction.atomic
1355    def bulk_create(self, *, portfolio_id: int, user, items: Sequence[Dict[str, Any]]) -> List[Section]:
1356        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, user=user)
1357        created: List[Section] = []
1358
1359        for item in items:
1360            self._reject_unknown_fields(item)
1361            created.append(self.create(portfolio_id=portfolio.id, user=user, validated_data=dict(item)))
1362
1363        return created
1364
1365    @transaction.atomic
1366    def bulk_update(self, *, portfolio_id: int, user, items: Sequence[Dict[str, Any]]) -> List[Section]:
1367        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, user=user)
1368        updated: List[Section] = []
1369
1370        for item in items:
1371            if not isinstance(item, dict):
1372                raise ValidationError({"detail": "Invalid bulk update payload."})
1373            section_id = item.get("id")
1374            if section_id is None:
1375                raise ValidationError({"id": "This field is required."})
1376            instance = self._resolve_section(portfolio_id=portfolio.id, section_id=int(section_id), user=user)
1377            payload = dict(item)
1378            payload.pop("id", None)
1379            self._reject_unknown_fields(payload)
1380            updated.append(self.update(instance=instance, user=user, validated_data=payload))
1381
1382        return updated
1383
1384
1385class BlockService:
1386    """Service managing blocks within a section."""
1387
1388    allowed_fields = {"type", "order", "is_visible", "config"}
1389    not_found_message = "Block not found."
1390    create_error_message = "Could not create block."
1391    update_error_message = "Could not update block."
1392
1393    def _normalize_type(self, value: Any) -> str:
1394        t = "" if value is None else str(value)
1395        t = t.strip().upper()
1396        if not t:
1397            raise ValidationError({"type": "Type cannot be empty."})
1398
1399        allowed = {c for c, _ in Block.BlockType.choices}
1400        if t not in allowed:
1401            raise ValidationError({"type": "Invalid block type."})
1402        return t
1403
1404    def _normalize_order(self, value: Any) -> int:
1405        try:
1406            order = int(value)
1407        except (TypeError, ValueError):
1408            raise ValidationError({"order": "Order must be an integer."})
1409
1410        if order < 1:
1411            raise ValidationError({"order": "Order must be >= 1."})
1412        return order
1413
1414    def _normalize_config(self, value: Any) -> Dict[str, Any]:
1415        if value is None:
1416            raise ValidationError({"config": "Config must be an object."})
1417        if not isinstance(value, dict):
1418            raise ValidationError({"config": "Config must be an object."})
1419        return value
1420
1421    def _reject_unknown_fields(self, validated_data: Any) -> None:
1422        keys = set(getattr(validated_data, "keys", lambda: [])())
1423        unknown = keys - self.allowed_fields
1424        if unknown:
1425            raise ValidationError({key: "Unknown field." for key in sorted(unknown)})
1426
1427    def _resolve_section(self, *, portfolio_id: int, section_id: int, user, for_update: bool = False) -> Section:
1428        qs = Section.objects.select_related("portfolio")
1429        if for_update:
1430            qs = qs.select_for_update()
1431        try:
1432            return qs.get(id=section_id, portfolio_id=portfolio_id, portfolio__user_id=user.id)
1433        except Section.DoesNotExist:
1434            raise NotFound("Section not found.")
1435
1436    def _resolve_block(self, *, portfolio_id: int, section_id: int, block_id: int, user, for_update: bool = False) -> Block:
1437        qs = Block.objects.select_related("section", "section__portfolio")
1438        if for_update:
1439            qs = qs.select_for_update()
1440        try:
1441            return qs.get(id=block_id, section_id=section_id, section__portfolio_id=portfolio_id, section__portfolio__user_id=user.id)
1442        except Block.DoesNotExist:
1443            raise NotFound(self.not_found_message)
1444
1445    def _lock_blocks(self, *, section: Section) -> List[Block]:
1446        return list(
1447            Block.objects.select_for_update()
1448            .filter(section=section)
1449            .order_by("order", "id")
1450        )
1451
1452    def _resequence_orders(self, *, blocks: List[Block]) -> None:
1453        changed: List[Block] = []
1454        expected = 1
1455        for b in blocks:
1456            if b.order != expected:
1457                b.order = expected
1458                changed.append(b)
1459            expected += 1
1460        if changed:
1461            Block.objects.bulk_update(changed, ["order"])
1462
1463    def _maybe_unpublish_if_invalid(self, *, portfolio: Portfolio) -> None:
1464        if not portfolio.is_published:
1465            return
1466
1467        has_section = Section.objects.filter(portfolio_id=portfolio.pk).exists()
1468        has_block = Block.objects.filter(section__portfolio_id=portfolio.pk).exists()
1469
1470        if has_section and has_block:
1471            return
1472
1473        portfolio.is_published = False
1474        portfolio.save(update_fields=["is_published", "updated_at"])
1475
1476    def _validate_grid_data_source_consistency(self, *, block: Block) -> None:
1477        if block.type != Block.BlockType.GRID:
1478            return
1479
1480        sources = {
1481            str(source).strip().upper()
1482            for source in Element.objects.select_for_update()
1483            .filter(block=block)
1484            .values_list("data_source", flat=True)
1485            if source is not None
1486        }
1487
1488        if len(sources) <= 1:
1489            return
1490
1491        raise ValidationError(
1492            {"type": "GRID blocks cannot mix element data sources. Use a single data source for all elements."}
1493        )
1494
1495    def list(self, *, portfolio_id: int, section_id: int, user) -> QuerySet[Block]:
1496        section = self._resolve_section(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, section_id=section_id, user=user)
1497        return Block.objects.filter(section=section).order_by("order", "id")
1498
1499    def retrieve(self, *, portfolio_id: int, section_id: int, block_id: int, user) -> Block:
1500        return self._resolve_block(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, section_id=section_id, block_id=block_id, user=user)
1501
1502    @transaction.atomic
1503    def create(self, *, portfolio_id: int, section_id: int, user, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Block:
1504        self._reject_unknown_fields(validated_data)
1505        section = self._resolve_section(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, section_id=section_id, user=user, for_update=True)
1506
1507        payload = dict(validated_data)
1508        if payload.get("type", None) is None:
1509            raise ValidationError({"type": "This field is required."})
1510
1511        payload["type"] = self._normalize_type(payload.get("type"))
1512        if "config" in payload:
1513            payload["config"] = self._normalize_config(payload.get("config"))
1514
1515        blocks = self._lock_blocks(section=section)
1516        self._resequence_orders(blocks=blocks)
1517
1518        desired_order = payload.get("order")
1519        if desired_order is None:
1520            desired_order = (blocks[-1].order + 1) if blocks else 1
1521        else:
1522            desired_order = self._normalize_order(desired_order)
1523
1524        max_allowed = len(blocks) + 1
1525        desired_order = max(1, min(int(desired_order), max_allowed))
1526        payload["order"] = desired_order
1527
1528        shifted: List[Block] = []
1529        for b in blocks:
1530            if b.order >= desired_order:
1531                b.order += 1
1532                shifted.append(b)
1533        if shifted:
1534            Block.objects.bulk_update(shifted, ["order"])
1535
1536        instance = Block(section=section, **payload)
1537
1538        try:
1539            instance.full_clean(exclude=None)
1540        except DjangoValidationError as exc:
1541            raise ValidationError(exc.message_dict or {"detail": exc.messages})
1542
1543        try:
1544            instance.save()
1545        except IntegrityError:
1546            raise ValidationError({"detail": self.create_error_message})
1547
1548        return instance
1549
1550    @transaction.atomic
1551    def update(self, *, instance: Block, user, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Block:
1552        self._reject_unknown_fields(validated_data)
1553
1554        # Re-resolve under owner scope and lock siblings in the same section.
1555        locked_instance = self._resolve_block(
1556            portfolio_id=instance.section.portfolio_id,
1557            section_id=instance.section_id,
1558            block_id=instance.id,
1559            user=user,
1560            for_update=True,
1561        )
1562        section = locked_instance.section
1563
1564        blocks = self._lock_blocks(section=section)
1565        self._resequence_orders(blocks=blocks)
1566
1567        current = next((b for b in blocks if b.id == locked_instance.id), None)
1568        if current is None:
1569            raise NotFound(self.not_found_message)
1570
1571        if "type" in validated_data:
1572            current.type = self._normalize_type(validated_data.get("type"))
1573            if current.type == Block.BlockType.GRID:
1574                self._validate_grid_data_source_consistency(block=current)
1575        if "config" in validated_data:
1576            current.config = self._normalize_config(validated_data.get("config"))
1577        if "is_visible" in validated_data:
1578            current.is_visible = validated_data.get("is_visible")
1579
1580        if "order" in validated_data:
1581            desired_order = self._normalize_order(validated_data.get("order"))
1582            desired_order = max(1, min(int(desired_order), len(blocks)))
1583            current_order = int(current.order)
1584
1585            if desired_order != current_order:
1586                affected: List[Block] = []
1587                if desired_order < current_order:
1588                    for b in blocks:
1589                        if b.id == current.id:
1590                            continue
1591                        if desired_order <= int(b.order) < current_order:
1592                            b.order = int(b.order) + 1
1593                            affected.append(b)
1594                else:
1595                    for b in blocks:
1596                        if b.id == current.id:
1597                            continue
1598                        if current_order < int(b.order) <= desired_order:
1599                            b.order = int(b.order) - 1
1600                            affected.append(b)
1601
1602                if affected:
1603                    Block.objects.bulk_update(affected, ["order"])
1604                current.order = desired_order
1605
1606        try:
1607            current.full_clean(exclude=None)
1608            current.save()
1609        except DjangoValidationError as exc:
1610            raise ValidationError(exc.message_dict or {"detail": exc.messages})
1611        except IntegrityError:
1612            raise ValidationError({"detail": self.update_error_message})
1613
1614        return current
1615
1616    @transaction.atomic
1617    def delete(self, *, instance: Block, user) -> None:
1618        locked_instance = self._resolve_block(
1619            portfolio_id=instance.section.portfolio_id,
1620            section_id=instance.section_id,
1621            block_id=instance.id,
1622            user=user,
1623            for_update=True,
1624        )
1625        section = locked_instance.section
1626        portfolio = section.portfolio
1627
1628        blocks = self._lock_blocks(section=section)
1629        self._resequence_orders(blocks=blocks)
1630
1631        target = next((b for b in blocks if b.id == locked_instance.id), None)
1632        if target is None:
1633            raise NotFound(self.not_found_message)
1634
1635        deleted_order = int(target.order)
1636        target.delete()
1637
1638        affected: List[Block] = []
1639        for b in blocks:
1640            if b.id == target.id:
1641                continue
1642            if int(b.order) > deleted_order:
1643                b.order = int(b.order) - 1
1644                affected.append(b)
1645        if affected:
1646            Block.objects.bulk_update(affected, ["order"])
1647
1648        self._maybe_unpublish_if_invalid(portfolio=portfolio)
1649
1650
1651class ElementService:
1652    """Service for managing elements (field mappings) for blocks."""
1653
1654    allowed_fields = {"label", "data_source", "field", "order", "is_visible", "config"}
1655    not_found_message = "Element not found."
1656    create_error_message = "Could not create element."
1657    update_error_message = "Could not update element."
1658
1659    def _normalize_order(self, value: Any) -> int:
1660        try:
1661            order = int(value)
1662        except (TypeError, ValueError):
1663            raise ValidationError({"order": "Order must be an integer."})
1664        if order < 1:
1665            raise ValidationError({"order": "Order must be >= 1."})
1666        return order
1667
1668    def _normalize_config(self, value: Any) -> Dict[str, Any]:
1669        if value is None:
1670            raise ValidationError({"config": "Config must be an object."})
1671        if not isinstance(value, dict):
1672            raise ValidationError({"config": "Config must be an object."})
1673        return value
1674
1675    def _reject_unknown_fields(self, validated_data: Any) -> None:
1676        keys = set(getattr(validated_data, "keys", lambda: [])())
1677        unknown = keys - self.allowed_fields
1678        if unknown:
1679            raise ValidationError({key: "Unknown field." for key in sorted(unknown)})
1680
1681    def _resolve_block(self, *, portfolio_id: int, section_id: int, block_id: int, user, for_update: bool = False) -> Block:
1682        qs = Block.objects.select_related("section", "section__portfolio")
1683        if for_update:
1684            qs = qs.select_for_update()
1685        try:
1686            return qs.get(
1687                id=block_id,
1688                section_id=section_id,
1689                section__portfolio_id=portfolio_id,
1690                section__portfolio__user_id=user.id,
1691            )
1692        except Block.DoesNotExist:
1693            raise NotFound("Block not found.")
1694
1695    def _resolve_element(
1696        self,
1697        *,
1698        portfolio_id: int,
1699        section_id: int,
1700        block_id: int,
1701        element_id: int,
1702        user,
1703        for_update: bool = False,
1704    ) -> Element:
1705        qs = Element.objects.select_related("block", "block__section", "block__section__portfolio")
1706        if for_update:
1707            qs = qs.select_for_update()
1708        try:
1709            return qs.get(
1710                id=element_id,
1711                block_id=block_id,
1712                block__section_id=section_id,
1713                block__section__portfolio_id=portfolio_id,
1714                block__section__portfolio__user_id=user.id,
1715            )
1716        except Element.DoesNotExist:
1717            raise NotFound(self.not_found_message)
1718
1719    def _lock_elements(self, *, block: Block) -> List[Element]:
1720        return list(
1721            Element.objects.select_for_update()
1722            .filter(block=block)
1723            .order_by("order", "id")
1724        )
1725
1726    def _resequence_orders(self, *, elements: List[Element]) -> None:
1727        changed: List[Element] = []
1728        expected = 1
1729        for e in elements:
1730            if e.order != expected:
1731                e.order = expected
1732                changed.append(e)
1733            expected += 1
1734        if changed:
1735            Element.objects.bulk_update(changed, ["order"])
1736
1737    def _validate_grid_data_source(
1738        self,
1739        *,
1740        block: Block,
1741        elements: List[Element],
1742        incoming_source: Any,
1743        exclude_element_id: int | None = None,
1744    ) -> None:
1745        if block.type != Block.BlockType.GRID:
1746            return
1747
1748        incoming = str(incoming_source or "").strip().upper()
1749        if not incoming:
1750            return
1751
1752        existing_sources = {
1753            str(e.data_source or "").strip().upper()
1754            for e in elements
1755            if e.id != exclude_element_id
1756        }
1757        existing_sources.discard("")
1758
1759        if len(existing_sources) > 1:
1760            raise ValidationError(
1761                {
1762                    "data_source": "GRID block already contains mixed data sources. Normalize existing elements to one source first."
1763                }
1764            )
1765
1766        if len(existing_sources) == 1:
1767            required = next(iter(existing_sources))
1768            if incoming != required:
1769                raise ValidationError(
1770                    {"data_source": f"All elements in a GRID block must use data source '{required}'."}
1771                )
1772
1773    def list(self, *, portfolio_id: int, section_id: int, block_id: int, user) -> QuerySet[Element]:
1774        block = self._resolve_block(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, section_id=section_id, block_id=block_id, user=user)
1775        return Element.objects.filter(block=block).order_by("order", "id")
1776
1777    def retrieve(self, *, portfolio_id: int, section_id: int, block_id: int, element_id: int, user) -> Element:
1778        return self._resolve_element(
1779            portfolio_id=portfolio_id,
1780            section_id=section_id,
1781            block_id=block_id,
1782            element_id=element_id,
1783            user=user,
1784        )
1785
1786    @transaction.atomic
1787    def create(
1788        self,
1789        *,
1790        portfolio_id: int,
1791        section_id: int,
1792        block_id: int,
1793        user,
1794        validated_data: Dict[str, Any],
1795    ) -> Element:
1796        self._reject_unknown_fields(validated_data)
1797        block = self._resolve_block(
1798            portfolio_id=portfolio_id,
1799            section_id=section_id,
1800            block_id=block_id,
1801            user=user,
1802            for_update=True,
1803        )
1804
1805        payload = dict(validated_data)
1806        if payload.get("label", None) is None:
1807            raise ValidationError({"label": "This field is required."})
1808        if payload.get("data_source", None) is None:
1809            raise ValidationError({"data_source": "This field is required."})
1810        if payload.get("field", None) is None:
1811            raise ValidationError({"field": "This field is required."})
1812
1813        if "config" in payload:
1814            payload["config"] = self._normalize_config(payload.get("config"))
1815
1816        elements = self._lock_elements(block=block)
1817        self._resequence_orders(elements=elements)
1818        self._validate_grid_data_source(
1819            block=block,
1820            elements=elements,
1821            incoming_source=payload.get("data_source"),
1822        )
1823
1824        desired_order = payload.get("order")
1825        if desired_order is None:
1826            desired_order = (elements[-1].order + 1) if elements else 1
1827        else:
1828            desired_order = self._normalize_order(desired_order)
1829
1830        max_allowed = len(elements) + 1
1831        desired_order = max(1, min(int(desired_order), max_allowed))
1832        payload["order"] = desired_order
1833
1834        shifted: List[Element] = []
1835        for e in elements:
1836            if e.order >= desired_order:
1837                e.order += 1
1838                shifted.append(e)
1839        if shifted:
1840            Element.objects.bulk_update(shifted, ["order"])
1841
1842        instance = Element(block=block, **payload)
1843        try:
1844            instance.full_clean(exclude=None)
1845        except DjangoValidationError as exc:
1846            raise ValidationError(exc.message_dict or {"detail": exc.messages})
1847
1848        try:
1849            instance.save()
1850        except IntegrityError:
1851            raise ValidationError({"detail": self.create_error_message})
1852
1853        return instance
1854
1855    @transaction.atomic
1856    def update(self, *, instance: Element, user, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Element:
1857        self._reject_unknown_fields(validated_data)
1858
1859        locked = self._resolve_element(
1860            portfolio_id=instance.block.section.portfolio_id,
1861            section_id=instance.block.section_id,
1862            block_id=instance.block_id,
1863            element_id=instance.id,
1864            user=user,
1865            for_update=True,
1866        )
1867        block = locked.block
1868
1869        elements = self._lock_elements(block=block)
1870        self._resequence_orders(elements=elements)
1871
1872        current = next((e for e in elements if e.id == locked.id), None)
1873        if current is None:
1874            raise NotFound(self.not_found_message)
1875
1876        if "label" in validated_data:
1877            current.label = validated_data.get("label")
1878        if "data_source" in validated_data:
1879            current.data_source = validated_data.get("data_source")
1880        if "field" in validated_data:
1881            current.field = validated_data.get("field")
1882        if "config" in validated_data:
1883            current.config = self._normalize_config(validated_data.get("config"))
1884        if "is_visible" in validated_data:
1885            current.is_visible = validated_data.get("is_visible")
1886
1887        if block.type == Block.BlockType.GRID and "data_source" in validated_data:
1888            self._validate_grid_data_source(
1889                block=block,
1890                elements=elements,
1891                incoming_source=current.data_source,
1892                exclude_element_id=current.id,
1893            )
1894
1895        if "order" in validated_data:
1896            desired_order = self._normalize_order(validated_data.get("order"))
1897            desired_order = max(1, min(int(desired_order), len(elements)))
1898            current_order = int(current.order)
1899
1900            if desired_order != current_order:
1901                affected: List[Element] = []
1902                if desired_order < current_order:
1903                    for e in elements:
1904                        if e.id == current.id:
1905                            continue
1906                        if desired_order <= int(e.order) < current_order:
1907                            e.order = int(e.order) + 1
1908                            affected.append(e)
1909                else:
1910                    for e in elements:
1911                        if e.id == current.id:
1912                            continue
1913                        if current_order < int(e.order) <= desired_order:
1914                            e.order = int(e.order) - 1
1915                            affected.append(e)
1916
1917                if affected:
1918                    Element.objects.bulk_update(affected, ["order"])
1919                current.order = desired_order
1920
1921        try:
1922            current.full_clean(exclude=None)
1923            current.save()
1924        except DjangoValidationError as exc:
1925            raise ValidationError(exc.message_dict or {"detail": exc.messages})
1926        except IntegrityError:
1927            raise ValidationError({"detail": self.update_error_message})
1928
1929        return current
1930
1931    @transaction.atomic
1932    def delete(self, *, instance: Element, user) -> None:
1933        locked = self._resolve_element(
1934            portfolio_id=instance.block.section.portfolio_id,
1935            section_id=instance.block.section_id,
1936            block_id=instance.block_id,
1937            element_id=instance.id,
1938            user=user,
1939            for_update=True,
1940        )
1941        block = locked.block
1942
1943        elements = self._lock_elements(block=block)
1944        self._resequence_orders(elements=elements)
1945
1946        target = next((e for e in elements if e.id == locked.id), None)
1947        if target is None:
1948            raise NotFound(self.not_found_message)
1949
1950        deleted_order = int(target.order)
1951        target.delete()
1952
1953        affected: List[Element] = []
1954        for e in elements:
1955            if e.id == target.id:
1956                continue
1957            if int(e.order) > deleted_order:
1958                e.order = int(e.order) - 1
1959                affected.append(e)
1960        if affected:
