1961            Element.objects.bulk_update(affected, ["order"])
1962
1963
1964class ProjectService(BasePortfolioChildService):
1965    model = Project
1966    allowed_fields = {"title", "description", "github_url", "image", "order", "is_visible"}
1967    not_found_message = "Project not found."
1968    create_error_message = "Could not create project."
1969    update_error_message = "Could not update project."
1970    ownership_error_message = "Project does not belong to this portfolio."
1971
1972    def retrieve(self, *, portfolio_id: int, project_id: int, user) -> Project:
1973        return super().retrieve(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, object_id=project_id, user=user)
1974
1975
1976class SkillService(BasePortfolioChildService):
1977    model = Skill
1978    allowed_fields = {"name", "level", "order", "is_visible"}
1979    not_found_message = "Skill not found."
1980    create_error_message = "Could not create skill."
1981    update_error_message = "Could not update skill."
1982    ownership_error_message = "Skill does not belong to this portfolio."
1983
1984    def retrieve(self, *, portfolio_id: int, skill_id: int, user) -> Skill:
1985        return super().retrieve(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, object_id=skill_id, user=user)

1988class ExperienceService(BasePortfolioChildService):
1989    model = Experience
1990    allowed_fields = {"company", "role", "timeline", "order", "is_visible"}
1991    not_found_message = "Experience not found."
1992    create_error_message = "Could not create experience."
1993    update_error_message = "Could not update experience."
1994    ownership_error_message = "Experience does not belong to this portfolio."
1995
1996    def retrieve(self, *, portfolio_id: int, experience_id: int, user) -> Experience:
1997        return super().retrieve(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, object_id=experience_id, user=user)
1998
1999
2000class AuthService:
2001    """Authentication helper used by the API (register/login)."""
2002
2003    def register(self, *, username: str, password: str, email: str | None = None) -> Dict[str, str]:
2004        """Create a new user and return JWT tokens.
2005
2006        Args:
2007            username: Desired username.
2008            password: Plain-text password to validate and store.
2009            email: Optional email address.
2010
2011        Returns:
2012            Dict with keys ``refresh`` and ``access`` containing JWT tokens.
2013
2014        Raises:
2015            rest_framework.exceptions.ValidationError: On validation or uniqueness errors.
2016        """
2017        User = get_user_model()
2018
2019        normalized_email = (email or "").strip() or None
2020
2021        if User.objects.filter(username=username).exists():
2022            raise ValidationError({"username": "A user with this username already exists."})
2023
2024        if normalized_email and User.objects.filter(email=normalized_email).exists():
2025            raise ValidationError({"email": "A user with this email already exists."})
2026
2027        try:
2028            validate_password(password)
2029        except DjangoValidationError as exc:
2030            raise ValidationError({"password": exc.messages})
2031
2032        try:
2033            user = User.objects.create_user(username=username, email=normalized_email, password=password)
2034        except IntegrityError:
2035            raise ValidationError({"detail": "Could not create user."})
2036
2037        refresh = RefreshToken.for_user(user)
2038        return {"refresh": str(refresh), "access": str(refresh.access_token)}
2039
2040    def login(self, *, username: str, password: str) -> Dict[str, str]:
2041        """Authenticate a user and return JWT tokens.
2042
2043        Args:
2044            username: Username to authenticate.
2045            password: Password to authenticate.
2046
2047        Returns:
2048            Dict with keys ``refresh`` and ``access`` containing JWT tokens.
2049
2050        Raises:
2051            rest_framework.exceptions.AuthenticationFailed: On invalid credentials or disabled account.
2052        """
2053        user = authenticate(username=username, password=password)
2054        if user is None:
2055            raise AuthenticationFailed("Invalid username or password")
2056
2057        if not user.is_active:
2058            raise AuthenticationFailed("User account is disabled")
2059
2060        refresh = RefreshToken.for_user(user)
2061        return {"refresh": str(refresh), "access": str(refresh.access_token)}
service_logger = <Logger portfolio.service (INFO)>
OPERATOR_MAP = {'eq': '', 'neq': '', 'gt': '__gt', 'gte': '__gte', 'lt': '__lt', 'lte': '__lte', 'contains': '__icontains', 'icontains': '__icontains', 'in': '__in'}
class PortfolioRenderService: 
342class PortfolioRenderService:
343    """Service responsible for producing the JSON-serializable portfolio payload.
344
345    This class encapsulates the rendering logic for both private (authenticated)
346    and public portfolio representations.
347    """
348
349    def _serialize_payload(self, *, portfolio: Portfolio, include_unpublished: bool) -> Dict[str, Any]:
350        """Serialize a Portfolio instance into a nested data payload.
351
352        Args:
353            portfolio: The Portfolio instance to serialize.
354            include_unpublished: Whether to include unpublished child records
355                when resolving element data.
356
357        Returns:
358            A dictionary representing the portfolio, suitable for JSON response.
359        """
360        theme_data = None
361        if portfolio.theme is not None:
362            theme_data = {
363                "name": portfolio.theme.name,
364                "config": _safe_config(portfolio.theme.config),
365            }
366
367        return {
368            "portfolio": {
369                "title": portfolio.title,
370                "slug": portfolio.slug,
371                "description": portfolio.description,
372                "resume": _serialize_value(portfolio.resume),
373                "theme": theme_data,
374                "sections": _fetch_sections(portfolio, include_unpublished=include_unpublished),
375            }
376        }
377
378    def render_portfolio(
379        self,
380        portfolio_id: int,
381        user_id: int,
382        *,
383        include_unpublished: bool = False,
384    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
385        """Render a portfolio owned by ``user_id``.
386
387        Args:
388            portfolio_id: ID of the portfolio to render.
389            user_id: ID of the owning user.
390            include_unpublished: If True, include unpublished children in payload.
391
392        Raises:
393            rest_framework.exceptions.NotFound: If the portfolio does not exist.
394
395        Returns:
396            Serialized portfolio payload as a dict.
397        """
398        qs = Portfolio.objects.select_related("theme", "user").filter(id=portfolio_id, user_id=user_id)
399        if not include_unpublished:
400            qs = qs.filter(is_published=True)
401
402        portfolio = qs.first()
403        if portfolio is None:
404            raise NotFound("Portfolio not found")
405
406        return self._serialize_payload(portfolio=portfolio, include_unpublished=include_unpublished)
407
408    def render_public_portfolio_by_slug(self, slug: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
409        """Render a public portfolio identified by ``slug``.
410
411        Args:
412            slug: Public slug string to look up.
413
414        Raises:
415            rest_framework.exceptions.NotFound: If no published portfolio matches.
416
417        Returns:
418            Serialized portfolio payload as a dict.
419        """
420        slug_value = str(slug or "").strip()
421        if not slug_value:
422            raise NotFound("Portfolio not found")
423
424        portfolio = (
425            Portfolio.objects.select_related("theme", "user")
426            .filter(slug=slug_value, is_published=True)
427            .order_by("-updated_at", "-id")
428            .first()
429        )
430        if portfolio is None:
431            raise NotFound("Portfolio not found")
432
433        return self._serialize_payload(portfolio=portfolio, include_unpublished=False)

Service responsible for producing the JSON-serializable portfolio payload.

This class encapsulates the rendering logic for both private (authenticated) and public portfolio representations.

def render_portfolio( self, portfolio_id: int, user_id: int, *, include_unpublished: bool = False) -> Dict[str, Any]: 
378    def render_portfolio(
379        self,
380        portfolio_id: int,
381        user_id: int,
382        *,
383        include_unpublished: bool = False,
384    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
385        """Render a portfolio owned by ``user_id``.
386
387        Args:
388            portfolio_id: ID of the portfolio to render.
389            user_id: ID of the owning user.
390            include_unpublished: If True, include unpublished children in payload.
391
392        Raises:
393            rest_framework.exceptions.NotFound: If the portfolio does not exist.
394
395        Returns:
396            Serialized portfolio payload as a dict.
397        """
398        qs = Portfolio.objects.select_related("theme", "user").filter(id=portfolio_id, user_id=user_id)
399        if not include_unpublished:
400            qs = qs.filter(is_published=True)
401
402        portfolio = qs.first()
403        if portfolio is None:
404            raise NotFound("Portfolio not found")
405
406        return self._serialize_payload(portfolio=portfolio, include_unpublished=include_unpublished)

Render a portfolio owned by user_id.

Args: portfolio_id: ID of the portfolio to render. user_id: ID of the owning user. include_unpublished: If True, include unpublished children in payload.

Raises: rest_framework.exceptions.NotFound: If the portfolio does not exist.

Returns: Serialized portfolio payload as a dict.

def render_public_portfolio_by_slug(self, slug: str) -> Dict[str, Any]: 
408    def render_public_portfolio_by_slug(self, slug: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
409        """Render a public portfolio identified by ``slug``.
410
411        Args:
412            slug: Public slug string to look up.
413
414        Raises:
415            rest_framework.exceptions.NotFound: If no published portfolio matches.
416
417        Returns:
418            Serialized portfolio payload as a dict.
419        """
420        slug_value = str(slug or "").strip()
421        if not slug_value:
422            raise NotFound("Portfolio not found")
423
424        portfolio = (
425            Portfolio.objects.select_related("theme", "user")
426            .filter(slug=slug_value, is_published=True)
427            .order_by("-updated_at", "-id")
428            .first()
429        )
430        if portfolio is None:
431            raise NotFound("Portfolio not found")
432
433        return self._serialize_payload(portfolio=portfolio, include_unpublished=False)

Render a public portfolio identified by slug.

Args: slug: Public slug string to look up.

Raises: rest_framework.exceptions.NotFound: If no published portfolio matches.

Returns: Serialized portfolio payload as a dict.

class PortfolioService: 
436class PortfolioService:
437    """Service for creating, updating and deleting portfolio aggregates.
438
439    This class contains validation, slug generation and publish-time checks
440    for Portfolio entities.
441    """
442
443    _ALLOWED_FIELDS = {"title", "slug", "description", "resume", "theme", "is_published"}
444
445    def list(self, *, user) -> QuerySet[Portfolio]:
446        """List portfolios owned by ``user``.
447
448        Args:
449            user: The user whose portfolios should be returned.
450
451        Returns:
452            QuerySet of :class:`Portfolio` ordered by creation time.
453        """
454        return Portfolio.objects.filter(user=user).select_related("theme").order_by("-created_at", "-id")
455
456    def retrieve(self, *, user, portfolio_id: int) -> Portfolio:
457        """Retrieve a portfolio by id for a given user.
458
459        Args:
460            user: Owner of the portfolio.
461            portfolio_id: ID of the portfolio to retrieve.
462
463        Raises:
464            rest_framework.exceptions.NotFound: If not found.
465
466        Returns:
467            The :class:`Portfolio` instance.
468        """
469        try:
470            return Portfolio.objects.select_related("theme").get(id=portfolio_id, user=user)
471        except Portfolio.DoesNotExist:
472            raise NotFound("Portfolio not found.")
473
474    def _ensure_owner(self, *, user, portfolio: Portfolio) -> None:
475        """Raise ``PermissionDenied`` when ``user`` is not the portfolio owner.
476
477        Args:
478            user: The requesting user.
479            portfolio: Portfolio instance being accessed.
480
481        Raises:
482            rest_framework.exceptions.PermissionDenied: If the user is not the owner.
483        """
484        if portfolio.user_id != user.id:
485            raise PermissionDenied("You do not have permission to access this portfolio.")
486
487    def _coerce_bool(self, value: Any, *, default: bool = False) -> bool:
488        """Coerce common truthy/falsy representations into a boolean.
489
490        Accepts booleans, numeric and string values ("true", "1", "yes", etc.).
491
492        Args:
493            value: Value to coerce.
494            default: Default boolean to return when ``value`` is ``None``.
495
496        Returns:
497            bool: Coerced boolean value.
498
499        Raises:
500            rest_framework.exceptions.ValidationError: If the value cannot be parsed.
501        """
502        if value is None:
503            return default
504        if isinstance(value, bool):
505            return value
506        if isinstance(value, (int, float)):
507            return bool(value)
508        if isinstance(value, str):
509            normalized = value.strip().lower()
510            if normalized in {"true", "1", "yes", "y", "on"}:
511                return True
512            if normalized in {"false", "0", "no", "n", "off"}:
513                return False
514        raise ValidationError({"is_published": "Invalid boolean value."})
515
516    def _slug_max_length(self) -> int:
517        """Return the configured max_length for the Portfolio.slug field.
518
519        Returns:
520            int: Maximum length (fallback 50).
521        """
522        field = Portfolio._meta.get_field("slug")
523        return int(getattr(field, "max_length", 50) or 50)
524
525    def _normalize_title(self, value: Any) -> str:
526        """Normalize and validate a portfolio title.
527
528        Args:
529            value: Title value to normalize.
530
531        Returns:
532            The normalized title string.
533
534        Raises:
535            rest_framework.exceptions.ValidationError: If the title is empty.
536        """
537        title = "" if value is None else str(value)
538        title = title.strip()
539        if not title:
540            raise ValidationError({"title": "Title cannot be empty."})
541        return title
542
543    def _normalize_slug_source(self, value: Any) -> str:
544        """Normalize a slug source string and apply ``slugify``.
545
546        Strips leading/trailing dashes from the slug.
547
548        Args:
549            value: Raw slug source value.
550
551        Returns:
552            Normalized slug string.
553        """
554        slug = "" if value is None else str(value)
555        slug = slug.strip()
556        slug = slugify(slug)
557        return slug.strip("-")
558
559    def _make_unique_slug(self, *, user, base_slug: str, exclude_portfolio_id: int | None = None) -> str:
560        """Generate a user-scoped unique slug based on ``base_slug``.
561
562        If the candidate slug already exists for the user, a numeric suffix is
563        appended to make it unique while respecting the field max length.
564
565        Args:
566            user: The owner user used to scope uniqueness checks.
567            base_slug: The normalized base slug to start from.
568            exclude_portfolio_id: Optional portfolio id to exclude from checks
569                (useful when updating an existing portfolio).
570
571        Returns:
572            A unique slug string for the user.
573
574        Raises:
575            rest_framework.exceptions.ValidationError: If the base_slug is empty.
576        """
577        max_len = self._slug_max_length()
578        base_slug = (base_slug or "").strip("-")[:max_len].strip("-")
579        if not base_slug:
580            raise ValidationError({"slug": "Slug cannot be empty."})
581
582        qs = Portfolio.objects.filter(user=user)
583        if exclude_portfolio_id is not None:
584            qs = qs.exclude(pk=exclude_portfolio_id)
585
586        candidate = base_slug
587        suffix = 0
588        while qs.filter(slug=candidate).exists():
589            suffix += 1
590            suffix_str = f"-{suffix}"
591            trimmed = base_slug[: max_len - len(suffix_str)].rstrip("-")
592            candidate = f"{trimmed}{suffix_str}"
593
594        return candidate
595
596    def _resolve_theme(self, value: Any) -> Theme | None:
597        """Resolve a theme reference to a :class:`Theme` instance.
598
599        Accepts a Theme instance, an integer id, or ``None``/empty string.
600
601        Args:
602            value: Theme id or :class:`Theme` instance.
603
604        Returns:
605            Theme or None.
606
607        Raises:
608            rest_framework.exceptions.ValidationError: On invalid references.
609        """
610        if value is None or value == "":
611            return None
612
613        if isinstance(value, Theme):
614            return value
615
616        try:
617            theme_id = int(value)
618        except (TypeError, ValueError):
619            raise ValidationError({"theme": "Invalid theme reference."})
620
621        try:
622            return Theme.objects.get(pk=theme_id)
623        except Theme.DoesNotExist:
624            raise ValidationError({"theme": "Theme not found."})
625
626    def _assert_publishable(self, portfolio: Portfolio) -> None:
627        """Validate that a portfolio can be published.
628
629        A publishable portfolio must be persisted and contain at least one
630        visible section and at least one block.
631
632        Raises:
633            rest_framework.exceptions.ValidationError: If the portfolio is not publishable.
634        """
635        if portfolio.pk is None:
636            raise ValidationError(
637                {
638                    "is_published": "Portfolio must be saved and have at least one section and at least one block before publishing."
639                }
640            )
641
642        has_section = Section.objects.filter(portfolio_id=portfolio.pk).exists()
643        has_block = Block.objects.filter(section__portfolio_id=portfolio.pk).exists()
644
645        if not has_section or not has_block:
646            raise ValidationError(
647                {
648                    "is_published": "Portfolio must have at least one section and at least one block before publishing."
649                }
650            )
651
652    def _reject_unknown_fields(self, payload: Any) -> None:
653        """Reject unexpected keys in payloads.
654
655        Args:
656            payload: A mapping-like object containing candidate fields.
657
658        Raises:
659            rest_framework.exceptions.ValidationError: If unknown fields are present.
660        """
661        keys = set(getattr(payload, "keys", lambda: [])())
662        unknown = keys - self._ALLOWED_FIELDS
663        if unknown:
664            raise ValidationError({key: "Unknown field." for key in sorted(unknown)})
665
666    @transaction.atomic
667    def create(self, *, user, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Portfolio:
668        """Create a new Portfolio aggregate.
669
670        Performs validation, slug normalization, and optional publish checks.
671
672        Args:
673            user: Owner of the new portfolio.
674            validated_data: Dict of validated input fields.
675
676        Returns:
677            The created :class:`Portfolio` instance.
678        """
679        service_logger.info(
680            "portfolio.create user_id=%s keys=%s",
681            getattr(user, "id", None),
682            sorted(list(validated_data.keys())),
683        )
684        self._reject_unknown_fields(validated_data)
685
686        if validated_data.get("title", None) is None:
687            raise ValidationError({"title": "This field is required."})
688
689        title = self._normalize_title(validated_data.get("title"))
690        description = validated_data.get("description", "")
691        if description is None:
692            description = ""
693        description = str(description)
694
695        raw_slug = validated_data.get("slug")
696        base_slug = (
697            self._normalize_slug_source(raw_slug)
698            if raw_slug not in (None, "")
699            else slugify(title)
700        )
701        slug = self._make_unique_slug(user=user, base_slug=base_slug)
702
703        theme = self._resolve_theme(validated_data.get("theme", None))
704        publish_requested = self._coerce_bool(validated_data.get("is_published", False), default=False)
705
706        # Create independently valid aggregate root (unpublished by default).
707        portfolio = Portfolio(
708            user=user,
709            title=title,
710            slug=slug,
711            description=description,
712            resume=validated_data.get("resume"),
713            theme=theme,
714            is_published=False,
715        )
716
717        try:
718            portfolio.full_clean(exclude=None)
719        except DjangoValidationError as exc:
720            raise ValidationError(exc.message_dict or {"detail": exc.messages})
721
722        try:
723            portfolio.save()
724        except IntegrityError:
725            raise ValidationError({"detail": "Could not create portfolio."})
726
727        # Only enforce child existence when publishing.
728        if publish_requested:
729            self._assert_publishable(portfolio)
730            portfolio.is_published = True
731            portfolio.save(update_fields=["is_published", "updated_at"])
732
733        return portfolio
734
735    @transaction.atomic
736    def update(self, *, user, instance: Portfolio, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Portfolio:
737        """Update a Portfolio aggregate with validated data.
738
739        Args:
740            user: Requesting user (must be owner).
741            instance: Portfolio instance to update.
742            validated_data: Dict of validated updates.
743
744        Returns:
745            Updated :class:`Portfolio` instance.
746        """
747        service_logger.info(
748            "portfolio.update user_id=%s portfolio_id=%s keys=%s",
749            getattr(user, "id", None),
750            getattr(instance, "id", None),
751            sorted(list(validated_data.keys())),
752        )
753        self._ensure_owner(user=user, portfolio=instance)
754
755        payload = validated_data
756        self._reject_unknown_fields(payload)
757
758        if "title" in payload:
759            instance.title = self._normalize_title(payload.get("title"))
760
761        if "description" in payload:
762            description = payload.get("description")
763            if description is None:
764                description = ""
765            instance.description = str(description)
766
767        if "resume" in payload:
768            instance.resume = payload.get("resume")
769
770        if "theme" in payload:
771            instance.theme = self._resolve_theme(payload.get("theme"))
772
773        if "slug" in payload:
774            raw_slug = payload.get("slug")
775            if raw_slug in (None, ""):
776                base_slug = slugify(instance.title)
777            else:
778                base_slug = self._normalize_slug_source(raw_slug)
779            instance.slug = self._make_unique_slug(
780                user=user,
781                base_slug=base_slug,
782                exclude_portfolio_id=instance.pk,
783            )
784
785        publish_requested = "is_published" in payload
786        if publish_requested:
787            new_is_published = self._coerce_bool(payload.get("is_published"), default=instance.is_published)
788            if new_is_published and not instance.is_published:
789                self._assert_publishable(instance)
790            instance.is_published = new_is_published
791
792        try:
793            instance.full_clean(exclude=None)
794            instance.save()
795        except DjangoValidationError as exc:
796            raise ValidationError(exc.message_dict or {"detail": exc.messages})
797        except IntegrityError:
798            raise ValidationError({"detail": "Could not update portfolio."})
799
800        return instance
801
802    @transaction.atomic
803    def delete(self, *, user, instance: Portfolio) -> None:
804        """Delete a portfolio after ensuring the requester is the owner.
805
806        Args:
807            user: Requesting user.
808            instance: Portfolio to delete.
809        """
810        service_logger.info(
811            "portfolio.delete user_id=%s portfolio_id=%s",
812            getattr(user, "id", None),
813            getattr(instance, "id", None),
814        )
815        self._ensure_owner(user=user, portfolio=instance)
816        instance.delete()

Service for creating, updating and deleting portfolio aggregates.

This class contains validation, slug generation and publish-time checks for Portfolio entities.

def list(self, *, user) -> django.db.models.query.QuerySet: 
445    def list(self, *, user) -> QuerySet[Portfolio]:
446        """List portfolios owned by ``user``.
447
448        Args:
449            user: The user whose portfolios should be returned.
450
451        Returns:
452            QuerySet of :class:`Portfolio` ordered by creation time.
453        """
454        return Portfolio.objects.filter(user=user).select_related("theme").order_by("-created_at", "-id")

List portfolios owned by user.

Args: user: The user whose portfolios should be returned.

Returns: QuerySet of Portfolio ordered by creation time.

def retrieve(self, *, user, portfolio_id: int) -> portfolio.models.Portfolio: 
456    def retrieve(self, *, user, portfolio_id: int) -> Portfolio:
457        """Retrieve a portfolio by id for a given user.
458
459        Args:
460            user: Owner of the portfolio.
461            portfolio_id: ID of the portfolio to retrieve.
462
463        Raises:
464            rest_framework.exceptions.NotFound: If not found.
465
466        Returns:
467            The :class:`Portfolio` instance.
468        """
469        try:
470            return Portfolio.objects.select_related("theme").get(id=portfolio_id, user=user)
471        except Portfolio.DoesNotExist:
472            raise NotFound("Portfolio not found.")

Retrieve a portfolio by id for a given user.

Args: user: Owner of the portfolio. portfolio_id: ID of the portfolio to retrieve.

Raises: rest_framework.exceptions.NotFound: If not found.

Returns: The Portfolio instance.

@transaction.atomic
def create( self, *, user, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> portfolio.models.Portfolio: 
666    @transaction.atomic
667    def create(self, *, user, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Portfolio:
668        """Create a new Portfolio aggregate.
669
670        Performs validation, slug normalization, and optional publish checks.
671
672        Args:
673            user: Owner of the new portfolio.
674            validated_data: Dict of validated input fields.
675
676        Returns:
677            The created :class:`Portfolio` instance.
678        """
679        service_logger.info(
680            "portfolio.create user_id=%s keys=%s",
681            getattr(user, "id", None),
682            sorted(list(validated_data.keys())),
683        )
684        self._reject_unknown_fields(validated_data)
685
686        if validated_data.get("title", None) is None:
687            raise ValidationError({"title": "This field is required."})
688
689        title = self._normalize_title(validated_data.get("title"))
690        description = validated_data.get("description", "")
691        if description is None:
692            description = ""
693        description = str(description)
694
695        raw_slug = validated_data.get("slug")
696        base_slug = (
697            self._normalize_slug_source(raw_slug)
698            if raw_slug not in (None, "")
699            else slugify(title)
700        )
701        slug = self._make_unique_slug(user=user, base_slug=base_slug)
702
703        theme = self._resolve_theme(validated_data.get("theme", None))
704        publish_requested = self._coerce_bool(validated_data.get("is_published", False), default=False)
705
706        # Create independently valid aggregate root (unpublished by default).
707        portfolio = Portfolio(
708            user=user,
709            title=title,
710            slug=slug,
711            description=description,
712            resume=validated_data.get("resume"),
713            theme=theme,
714            is_published=False,
715        )
716
717        try:
718            portfolio.full_clean(exclude=None)
719        except DjangoValidationError as exc:
720            raise ValidationError(exc.message_dict or {"detail": exc.messages})
721
722        try:
723            portfolio.save()
724        except IntegrityError:
725            raise ValidationError({"detail": "Could not create portfolio."})
726
727        # Only enforce child existence when publishing.
728        if publish_requested:
729            self._assert_publishable(portfolio)
730            portfolio.is_published = True
731            portfolio.save(update_fields=["is_published", "updated_at"])
732
733        return portfolio

Create a new Portfolio aggregate.

Performs validation, slug normalization, and optional publish checks.

Args: user: Owner of the new portfolio. validated_data: Dict of validated input fields.

Returns: The created Portfolio instance.

@transaction.atomic
def update( self, *, user, instance: portfolio.models.Portfolio, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> portfolio.models.Portfolio: 
735    @transaction.atomic
736    def update(self, *, user, instance: Portfolio, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Portfolio:
737        """Update a Portfolio aggregate with validated data.
738
739        Args:
740            user: Requesting user (must be owner).
741            instance: Portfolio instance to update.
742            validated_data: Dict of validated updates.
743
744        Returns:
745            Updated :class:`Portfolio` instance.
746        """
747        service_logger.info(
748            "portfolio.update user_id=%s portfolio_id=%s keys=%s",
749            getattr(user, "id", None),
750            getattr(instance, "id", None),
751            sorted(list(validated_data.keys())),
752        )
753        self._ensure_owner(user=user, portfolio=instance)
754
755        payload = validated_data
756        self._reject_unknown_fields(payload)
757
758        if "title" in payload:
759            instance.title = self._normalize_title(payload.get("title"))
760
761        if "description" in payload:
762            description = payload.get("description")
763            if description is None:
764                description = ""
765            instance.description = str(description)
766
767        if "resume" in payload:
768            instance.resume = payload.get("resume")
769
770        if "theme" in payload:
771            instance.theme = self._resolve_theme(payload.get("theme"))
772
773        if "slug" in payload:
774            raw_slug = payload.get("slug")
775            if raw_slug in (None, ""):
776                base_slug = slugify(instance.title)
777            else:
778                base_slug = self._normalize_slug_source(raw_slug)
779            instance.slug = self._make_unique_slug(
780                user=user,
781                base_slug=base_slug,
782                exclude_portfolio_id=instance.pk,
783            )
784
785        publish_requested = "is_published" in payload
786        if publish_requested:
787            new_is_published = self._coerce_bool(payload.get("is_published"), default=instance.is_published)
788            if new_is_published and not instance.is_published:
789                self._assert_publishable(instance)
790            instance.is_published = new_is_published
791
792        try:
793            instance.full_clean(exclude=None)
794            instance.save()
795        except DjangoValidationError as exc:
796            raise ValidationError(exc.message_dict or {"detail": exc.messages})
797        except IntegrityError:
798            raise ValidationError({"detail": "Could not update portfolio."})
799
800        return instance

Update a Portfolio aggregate with validated data.

Args: user: Requesting user (must be owner). instance: Portfolio instance to update. validated_data: Dict of validated updates.

Returns: Updated Portfolio instance.

@transaction.atomic
def delete(self, *, user, instance: portfolio.models.Portfolio) -> None: 
802    @transaction.atomic
803    def delete(self, *, user, instance: Portfolio) -> None:
804        """Delete a portfolio after ensuring the requester is the owner.
805
806        Args:
807            user: Requesting user.
808            instance: Portfolio to delete.
809        """
810        service_logger.info(
811            "portfolio.delete user_id=%s portfolio_id=%s",
812            getattr(user, "id", None),
813            getattr(instance, "id", None),
814        )
815        self._ensure_owner(user=user, portfolio=instance)
816        instance.delete()

Delete a portfolio after ensuring the requester is the owner.

Args: user: Requesting user. instance: Portfolio to delete.

class BasePortfolioChildService: 
 819class BasePortfolioChildService:
 820    """Base helper for services that manage portfolio-scoped child models.
 821
 822    Provides common ordering, creation and update patterns used by
 823    :class:`ProjectService`, :class:`SkillService`, :class:`ExperienceService`,
 824    :class:`SectionService`, :class:`BlockService` and :class:`ElementService`.
 825    """
 826
 827    model = None
 828    allowed_fields: set[str] = set()
 829    not_found_message = "Object not found."
 830    create_error_message = "Could not create object."
 831    update_error_message = "Could not update object."
 832    ownership_error_message = "Object does not belong to this portfolio."
 833
 834    def _resolve_portfolio(self, *, portfolio_id: int, user, for_update: bool = False) -> Portfolio:
 835        """Resolve a portfolio for owner-scoped operations.
 836
 837        Args:
 838            portfolio_id: Portfolio id to resolve.
 839            user: Requesting user.
 840            for_update: If True, use ``select_for_update`` to lock rows.
 841
 842        Raises:
 843            rest_framework.exceptions.NotFound: If portfolio is not found.
 844
 845        Returns:
 846            The resolved :class:`Portfolio` instance.
 847        """
 848        qs = Portfolio.objects
 849        if for_update:
 850            qs = qs.select_for_update()
 851
 852        portfolio = qs.filter(id=portfolio_id, user_id=user.id).first()
 853        if portfolio is None:
 854            raise NotFound("Portfolio not found.")
 855
 856        return portfolio
 857
 858    def _resolve_instance(self, *, portfolio_id: int, object_id: int, user, for_update: bool = False):
 859        """Resolve a child instance belonging to a portfolio.
 860
 861        Args:
 862            portfolio_id: Owning portfolio id.
 863            object_id: The child's id.
 864            user: Requesting user.
 865            for_update: Use ``select_for_update`` when True.
 866
 867        Raises:
 868            rest_framework.exceptions.NotFound: If the object does not exist.
 869
 870        Returns:
 871            The model instance.
 872        """
 873        qs = self.model.objects.select_related("portfolio")
 874        if for_update:
 875            qs = qs.select_for_update()
 876
 877        try:
 878            return qs.get(id=object_id, portfolio_id=portfolio_id, portfolio__user_id=user.id)
 879        except self.model.DoesNotExist:
 880            raise NotFound(self.not_found_message)
 881
 882    def _reject_unknown_fields(self, validated_data: Any) -> None:
 883        """Reject unknown payload keys based on ``allowed_fields``.
 884
 885        Args:
 886            validated_data: Mapping-like object of payload fields.
 887
 888        Raises:
 889            rest_framework.exceptions.ValidationError: If unknown keys exist.
 890        """
 891        keys = set(getattr(validated_data, "keys", lambda: [])())
 892        unknown = keys - self.allowed_fields
 893        if unknown:
 894            raise ValidationError({key: "Unknown field." for key in sorted(unknown)})
 895
 896    def _get_queryset(self, portfolio: Portfolio) -> QuerySet:
 897        """Return a QuerySet for this service's ``model`` scoped to ``portfolio``."""
 898        return self.model.objects.filter(portfolio=portfolio)
 899
 900    def _coerce_order(self, value: Any) -> int:
 901        """Coerce and validate an order value to a positive integer.
 902
 903        Raises ValidationError for invalid or missing values.
 904        """
 905        if value is None:
 906            raise ValidationError({"order": "Order cannot be null."})
 907
 908        try:
 909            order = int(value)
 910        except (TypeError, ValueError):
 911            raise ValidationError({"order": "Order must be an integer."})
 912
 913        return max(1, order)
 914
 915    def _lock_siblings(self, *, portfolio: Portfolio) -> List[Any]:
 916        """Lock and return sibling instances ordered by order,id for mutation."""
 917        return list(
 918            self._get_queryset(portfolio)
 919            .select_for_update()
 920            .order_by("order", "id")
 921        )
 922
 923    def _resequence_orders(self, *, siblings: List[Any]) -> None:
 924        """Normalize ordering to a contiguous 1..n sequence.
 925
 926        Bulk-updates any changed order values.
 927        """
 928        changed: List[Any] = []
 929        expected = 1
 930        for instance in siblings:
 931            if int(getattr(instance, "order", 0)) != expected:
 932                instance.order = expected
 933                changed.append(instance)
 934            expected += 1
 935
 936        if changed:
 937            self.model.objects.bulk_update(changed, ["order"])
 938
 939    def _with_auto_order(self, *, portfolio: Portfolio, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 940        """Return a payload that fills an ``order`` if missing based on siblings."""
 941        payload = dict(validated_data)
 942        if "order" not in payload or payload.get("order") is None:
 943            last_order = (
 944                self._get_queryset(portfolio)
 945                .order_by("-order", "-id")
 946                .values_list("order", flat=True)
 947                .first()
 948            )
 949            payload["order"] = (last_order + 1) if last_order is not None else 1
 950        return payload
 951
 952    def list(self, *, portfolio_id: int, user) -> QuerySet:
 953        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, user=user)
 954        return self._get_queryset(portfolio).order_by("order", "id")
 955
 956    def retrieve(self, *, portfolio_id: int, object_id: int, user):
 957        return self._resolve_instance(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, object_id=object_id, user=user)
 958
 959    @transaction.atomic
 960    def create(self, *, portfolio_id: int, user, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]):
 961        self._reject_unknown_fields(validated_data)
 962        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, user=user)
 963        payload = dict(validated_data)
 964
 965        siblings = self._lock_siblings(portfolio=portfolio)
 966        self._resequence_orders(siblings=siblings)
 967
 968        desired_order = payload.get("order")
 969        if desired_order is None:
 970            desired_order = (siblings[-1].order + 1) if siblings else 1
 971        else:
 972            desired_order = self._coerce_order(desired_order)
 973
 974        max_allowed = len(siblings) + 1
 975        desired_order = max(1, min(int(desired_order), max_allowed))
 976        payload["order"] = desired_order
 977
 978        shifted: List[Any] = []
 979        for sibling in siblings:
 980            if int(getattr(sibling, "order", 0)) >= desired_order:
 981                sibling.order = int(getattr(sibling, "order", 0)) + 1
 982                shifted.append(sibling)
 983
 984        if shifted:
 985            self.model.objects.bulk_update(shifted, ["order"])
 986
 987        instance = self.model(portfolio=portfolio, **payload)
 988
 989        try:
 990            instance.full_clean(exclude=None)
 991        except DjangoValidationError as exc:
 992            raise ValidationError(exc.message_dict or {"detail": exc.messages})
 993
 994        try:
 995            instance.save()
 996        except IntegrityError:
 997            raise ValidationError({"detail": self.create_error_message})
 998
 999        return instance
1000
1001    @transaction.atomic
1002    def update(self, *, instance, user, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]):
1003        self._reject_unknown_fields(validated_data)
1004        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=instance.portfolio_id, user=user)
1005
1006        siblings = self._lock_siblings(portfolio=portfolio)
1007        self._resequence_orders(siblings=siblings)
1008
1009        locked_instance = next((s for s in siblings if s.id == instance.id), None)
1010        if locked_instance is None:
1011            raise NotFound(self.not_found_message)
1012
1013        desired_order: int | None = None
1014        if "order" in validated_data:
1015            desired_order = self._coerce_order(validated_data.get("order"))
1016            desired_order = max(1, min(int(desired_order), len(siblings)))
1017
1018        for key, value in validated_data.items():
1019            if key == "order":
1020                continue
1021            setattr(locked_instance, key, value)
1022
1023        if desired_order is not None:
1024            current_order = int(getattr(locked_instance, "order", 1))
1025            if desired_order != current_order:
1026                affected: List[Any] = []
1027                if desired_order < current_order:
1028                    for sibling in siblings:
1029                        if sibling.id == locked_instance.id:
1030                            continue
1031                        if desired_order <= int(getattr(sibling, "order", 0)) < current_order:
1032                            sibling.order = int(getattr(sibling, "order", 0)) + 1
1033                            affected.append(sibling)
1034                else:
1035                    for sibling in siblings:
1036                        if sibling.id == locked_instance.id:
1037                            continue
1038                        if current_order < int(getattr(sibling, "order", 0)) <= desired_order:
1039                            sibling.order = int(getattr(sibling, "order", 0)) - 1
1040                            affected.append(sibling)
1041
1042                if affected:
1043                    self.model.objects.bulk_update(affected, ["order"])
1044                locked_instance.order = desired_order
1045
1046        try:
1047            locked_instance.full_clean(exclude=None)
1048            locked_instance.save()
1049        except DjangoValidationError as exc:
1050            raise ValidationError(exc.message_dict or {"detail": exc.messages})
1051        except IntegrityError:
1052            raise ValidationError({"detail": self.update_error_message})
1053
1054        return locked_instance
1055
1056    @transaction.atomic
1057    def delete(self, *, instance, user) -> None:
1058        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=instance.portfolio_id, user=user)
1059
1060        siblings = self._lock_siblings(portfolio=portfolio)
1061        self._resequence_orders(siblings=siblings)
1062
1063        locked_instance = next((s for s in siblings if s.id == instance.id), None)
1064        if locked_instance is None:
1065            raise NotFound(self.not_found_message)
1066
1067        deleted_order = int(getattr(locked_instance, "order", 1))
1068        locked_instance.delete()
1069
1070        affected: List[Any] = []
1071        for sibling in siblings:
1072            if sibling.id == locked_instance.id:
1073                continue
1074            if int(getattr(sibling, "order", 0)) > deleted_order:
1075                sibling.order = int(getattr(sibling, "order", 0)) - 1
1076                affected.append(sibling)
1077
1078        if affected:
1079            self.model.objects.bulk_update(affected, ["order"])

Base helper for services that manage portfolio-scoped child models.

Provides common ordering, creation and update patterns used by ProjectService, SkillService, ExperienceService, SectionService, BlockService and ElementService.

model = None
allowed_fields: set[str] = set()
not_found_message = 'Object not found.'
create_error_message = 'Could not create object.'
update_error_message = 'Could not update object.'
ownership_error_message = 'Object does not belong to this portfolio.'
def list(self, *, portfolio_id: int, user) -> django.db.models.query.QuerySet: 
952    def list(self, *, portfolio_id: int, user) -> QuerySet:
953        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, user=user)
954        return self._get_queryset(portfolio).order_by("order", "id")
def retrieve(self, *, portfolio_id: int, object_id: int, user): 
956    def retrieve(self, *, portfolio_id: int, object_id: int, user):
957        return self._resolve_instance(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, object_id=object_id, user=user)
@transaction.atomic
def create(self, *, portfolio_id: int, user, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]): 
959    @transaction.atomic
960    def create(self, *, portfolio_id: int, user, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]):
961        self._reject_unknown_fields(validated_data)
962        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, user=user)
963        payload = dict(validated_data)
964
965        siblings = self._lock_siblings(portfolio=portfolio)
966        self._resequence_orders(siblings=siblings)
967
968        desired_order = payload.get("order")
969        if desired_order is None:
970            desired_order = (siblings[-1].order + 1) if siblings else 1
971        else:
972            desired_order = self._coerce_order(desired_order)
973
974        max_allowed = len(siblings) + 1
975        desired_order = max(1, min(int(desired_order), max_allowed))
976        payload["order"] = desired_order
977
978        shifted: List[Any] = []
979        for sibling in siblings:
980            if int(getattr(sibling, "order", 0)) >= desired_order:
981                sibling.order = int(getattr(sibling, "order", 0)) + 1
982                shifted.append(sibling)
983
984        if shifted:
985            self.model.objects.bulk_update(shifted, ["order"])
986
987        instance = self.model(portfolio=portfolio, **payload)
988
989        try:
990            instance.full_clean(exclude=None)
991        except DjangoValidationError as exc:
992            raise ValidationError(exc.message_dict or {"detail": exc.messages})
993
994        try:
995            instance.save()
996        except IntegrityError:
997            raise ValidationError({"detail": self.create_error_message})
998
999        return instance
@transaction.atomic
def update(self, *, instance, user, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]): 
1001    @transaction.atomic
1002    def update(self, *, instance, user, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]):
1003        self._reject_unknown_fields(validated_data)
1004        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=instance.portfolio_id, user=user)
1005
1006        siblings = self._lock_siblings(portfolio=portfolio)
1007        self._resequence_orders(siblings=siblings)
1008
1009        locked_instance = next((s for s in siblings if s.id == instance.id), None)
1010        if locked_instance is None:
1011            raise NotFound(self.not_found_message)
1012
1013        desired_order: int | None = None
1014        if "order" in validated_data:
1015            desired_order = self._coerce_order(validated_data.get("order"))
1016            desired_order = max(1, min(int(desired_order), len(siblings)))
1017
1018        for key, value in validated_data.items():
1019            if key == "order":
1020                continue
1021            setattr(locked_instance, key, value)
1022
1023        if desired_order is not None:
1024            current_order = int(getattr(locked_instance, "order", 1))
1025            if desired_order != current_order:
1026                affected: List[Any] = []
1027                if desired_order < current_order:
1028                    for sibling in siblings:
1029                        if sibling.id == locked_instance.id:
1030                            continue
1031                        if desired_order <= int(getattr(sibling, "order", 0)) < current_order:
1032                            sibling.order = int(getattr(sibling, "order", 0)) + 1
1033                            affected.append(sibling)
1034                else:
1035                    for sibling in siblings:
1036                        if sibling.id == locked_instance.id:
1037                            continue
1038                        if current_order < int(getattr(sibling, "order", 0)) <= desired_order:
1039                            sibling.order = int(getattr(sibling, "order", 0)) - 1
1040                            affected.append(sibling)
1041
1042                if affected:
1043                    self.model.objects.bulk_update(affected, ["order"])
1044                locked_instance.order = desired_order
1045
1046        try:
1047            locked_instance.full_clean(exclude=None)
1048            locked_instance.save()
1049        except DjangoValidationError as exc:
1050            raise ValidationError(exc.message_dict or {"detail": exc.messages})
1051        except IntegrityError:
1052            raise ValidationError({"detail": self.update_error_message})
1053
1054        return locked_instance
@transaction.atomic
def delete(self, *, instance, user) -> None: 
1056    @transaction.atomic
1057    def delete(self, *, instance, user) -> None:
1058        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=instance.portfolio_id, user=user)
1059
1060        siblings = self._lock_siblings(portfolio=portfolio)
1061        self._resequence_orders(siblings=siblings)
1062
1063        locked_instance = next((s for s in siblings if s.id == instance.id), None)
1064        if locked_instance is None:
1065            raise NotFound(self.not_found_message)
1066
1067        deleted_order = int(getattr(locked_instance, "order", 1))
1068        locked_instance.delete()
1069
1070        affected: List[Any] = []
1071        for sibling in siblings:
1072            if sibling.id == locked_instance.id:
1073                continue
1074            if int(getattr(sibling, "order", 0)) > deleted_order:
1075                sibling.order = int(getattr(sibling, "order", 0)) - 1
1076                affected.append(sibling)
1077
1078        if affected:
1079            self.model.objects.bulk_update(affected, ["order"])
class SectionService: 
1082class SectionService:
1083    """Service managing portfolio sections and their ordering."""
1084
1085    allowed_fields = {"name", "order", "is_visible", "config"}
1086    not_found_message = "Section not found."
1087    create_error_message = "Could not create section."
1088    update_error_message = "Could not update section."
1089
1090    def _normalize_name(self, value: Any) -> str:
1091        name = "" if value is None else str(value)
1092        name = name.strip()
1093        if not name:
1094            raise ValidationError({"name": "Name cannot be empty."})
1095        return name
1096
1097    def _normalize_order(self, value: Any) -> int:
1098        try:
1099            order = int(value)
1100        except (TypeError, ValueError):
1101            raise ValidationError({"order": "Order must be an integer."})
1102
1103        # Sections are typically 1-based in the seeded dataset and UI ordering.
1104        if order < 1:
1105            raise ValidationError({"order": "Order must be >= 1."})
1106        return order
1107
1108    def _normalize_config(self, value: Any) -> Dict[str, Any]:
1109        if value is None:
1110            raise ValidationError({"config": "Config must be an object."})
1111        if not isinstance(value, dict):
1112            raise ValidationError({"config": "Config must be an object."})
1113        return value
1114
1115    def _resolve_portfolio(self, *, portfolio_id: int, user, for_update: bool = False) -> Portfolio:
1116        qs = Portfolio.objects
1117        if for_update:
1118            qs = qs.select_for_update()
1119        portfolio = qs.filter(id=portfolio_id, user_id=user.id).first()
1120        if portfolio is None:
1121            raise NotFound("Portfolio not found.")
1122        return portfolio
1123
1124    def _resolve_section(self, *, portfolio_id: int, section_id: int, user, for_update: bool = False) -> Section:
1125        qs = Section.objects.select_related("portfolio")
1126        if for_update:
1127            qs = qs.select_for_update()
1128        try:
1129            return qs.get(id=section_id, portfolio_id=portfolio_id, portfolio__user_id=user.id)
1130        except Section.DoesNotExist:
1131            raise NotFound(self.not_found_message)
1132
1133    def _reject_unknown_fields(self, validated_data: Any) -> None:
1134        keys = set(getattr(validated_data, "keys", lambda: [])())
1135        unknown = keys - self.allowed_fields
1136        if unknown:
1137            raise ValidationError({key: "Unknown field." for key in sorted(unknown)})
1138
1139    def _lock_sections(self, *, portfolio: Portfolio) -> List[Section]:
1140        return list(
1141            Section.objects.select_for_update()
1142            .filter(portfolio=portfolio)
1143            .order_by("order", "id")
1144        )
1145
1146    def _resequence_orders(self, *, sections: List[Section]) -> None:
1147        """Force stable, contiguous ordering (1..n) within a portfolio."""
1148
1149        changed: List[Section] = []
1150        expected = 1
1151        for section in sections:
1152            if section.order != expected:
1153                section.order = expected
1154                changed.append(section)
1155            expected += 1
1156
1157        if changed:
1158            Section.objects.bulk_update(changed, ["order"])
1159
1160    def _maybe_unpublish_if_invalid(self, *, portfolio: Portfolio) -> None:
1161        if not portfolio.is_published:
1162            return
1163
1164        has_section = Section.objects.filter(portfolio_id=portfolio.pk).exists()
1165        has_block = Block.objects.filter(section__portfolio_id=portfolio.pk).exists()
1166
1167        if has_section and has_block:
1168            return
1169
1170        portfolio.is_published = False
1171        portfolio.save(update_fields=["is_published", "updated_at"])
1172
1173    def list(self, *, portfolio_id: int, user) -> QuerySet[Section]:
1174        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, user=user)
1175        return Section.objects.filter(portfolio=portfolio).order_by("order", "id")
1176
1177    def retrieve(self, *, portfolio_id: int, section_id: int, user) -> Section:
1178        return self._resolve_section(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, section_id=section_id, user=user)
1179
1180    @transaction.atomic
1181    def create(self, *, portfolio_id: int, user, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Section:
1182        self._reject_unknown_fields(validated_data)
1183        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, user=user)
1184
1185        payload = dict(validated_data)
1186
1187        if payload.get("name", None) is None:
1188            raise ValidationError({"name": "This field is required."})
1189
1190        payload["name"] = self._normalize_name(payload.get("name"))
1191        if "config" in payload:
1192            payload["config"] = self._normalize_config(payload.get("config"))
1193
1194        sections = self._lock_sections(portfolio=portfolio)
1195        self._resequence_orders(sections=sections)
1196
1197        desired_order = payload.get("order")
1198        if desired_order is None:
1199            desired_order = (sections[-1].order + 1) if sections else 1
1200        else:
1201            desired_order = self._normalize_order(desired_order)
1202
1203        max_allowed = len(sections) + 1
1204        desired_order = max(1, min(int(desired_order), max_allowed))
1205        payload["order"] = desired_order
1206
1207        # Insert behavior: shift existing sections down.
1208        shifted: List[Section] = []
1209        for section in sections:
1210            if section.order >= desired_order:
1211                section.order += 1
1212                shifted.append(section)
1213        if shifted:
1214            Section.objects.bulk_update(shifted, ["order"])
1215
1216        instance = Section(portfolio=portfolio, **payload)
1217
1218        try:
1219            instance.full_clean(exclude=None)
1220        except DjangoValidationError as exc:
1221            raise ValidationError(exc.message_dict or {"detail": exc.messages})
1222
1223        try:
1224            instance.save()
1225        except IntegrityError:
1226            raise ValidationError({"detail": self.create_error_message})
1227
1228        return instance
1229
1230    @transaction.atomic
1231    def update(self, *, instance: Section, user, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Section:
1232        self._reject_unknown_fields(validated_data)
1233        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=instance.portfolio_id, user=user)
1234
1235        sections = self._lock_sections(portfolio=portfolio)
1236        self._resequence_orders(sections=sections)
1237
1238        locked_instance = next((s for s in sections if s.id == instance.id), None)
1239        if locked_instance is None:
1240            raise NotFound(self.not_found_message)
1241
1242        if "name" in validated_data:
1243            locked_instance.name = self._normalize_name(validated_data.get("name"))
1244        if "config" in validated_data:
1245            locked_instance.config = self._normalize_config(validated_data.get("config"))
1246        if "is_visible" in validated_data:
1247            locked_instance.is_visible = validated_data.get("is_visible")
1248
1249        if "order" in validated_data:
1250            desired_order = self._normalize_order(validated_data.get("order"))
1251            desired_order = max(1, min(int(desired_order), len(sections)))
1252            current_order = int(locked_instance.order)
1253
1254            if desired_order != current_order:
1255                affected: List[Section] = []
1256                if desired_order < current_order:
1257                    for section in sections:
1258                        if section.id == locked_instance.id:
1259                            continue
1260                        if desired_order <= int(section.order) < current_order:
1261                            section.order = int(section.order) + 1
1262                            affected.append(section)
1263                else:
1264                    for section in sections:
1265                        if section.id == locked_instance.id:
1266                            continue
1267                        if current_order < int(section.order) <= desired_order:
1268                            section.order = int(section.order) - 1
1269                            affected.append(section)
1270
1271                if affected:
1272                    Section.objects.bulk_update(affected, ["order"])
1273                locked_instance.order = desired_order
1274
1275        try:
1276            locked_instance.full_clean(exclude=None)
1277            locked_instance.save()
1278        except DjangoValidationError as exc:
1279            raise ValidationError(exc.message_dict or {"detail": exc.messages})
1280        except IntegrityError:
1281            raise ValidationError({"detail": self.update_error_message})
1282
1283        return locked_instance
1284
1285    @transaction.atomic
1286    def delete(self, *, instance: Section, user) -> None:
1287        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=instance.portfolio_id, user=user)
1288
1289        sections = self._lock_sections(portfolio=portfolio)
1290        self._resequence_orders(sections=sections)
1291
1292        locked_instance = next((s for s in sections if s.id == instance.id), None)
1293        if locked_instance is None:
1294            raise NotFound(self.not_found_message)
1295
1296        deleted_order = int(locked_instance.order)
1297        locked_instance.delete()
1298
1299        affected: List[Section] = []
1300        for section in sections:
1301            if section.id == locked_instance.id:
1302                continue
1303            if int(section.order) > deleted_order:
1304                section.order = int(section.order) - 1
1305                affected.append(section)
1306
1307        if affected:
1308            Section.objects.bulk_update(affected, ["order"])
1309
1310        self._maybe_unpublish_if_invalid(portfolio=portfolio)
1311
1312    @transaction.atomic
1313    def reorder(self, *, portfolio_id: int, user, order_pairs: Sequence[tuple[int, int]]) -> List[Section]:
1314        """Reorder sections using (section_id, desired_order) pairs.
1315
1316        Applies a stable sort and then resequences to contiguous 1..n.
1317        """
1318
1319        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, user=user)
1320        sections = self._lock_sections(portfolio=portfolio)
1321        self._resequence_orders(sections=sections)
1322
1323        desired_map: Dict[int, int] = {}
1324        for section_id, desired_order in order_pairs:
1325            try:
1326                desired_map[int(section_id)] = self._normalize_order(desired_order)
1327            except (TypeError, ValueError):
1328                raise ValidationError({"order": "Invalid reorder payload."})
1329
1330        ids = {s.id for s in sections}
1331        unknown = sorted([sid for sid in desired_map.keys() if sid not in ids])
1332        if unknown:
1333            raise ValidationError({"detail": "One or more sections do not exist."})
1334
1335        def sort_key(section: Section) -> tuple:
1336            desired = desired_map.get(section.id)
1337            if desired is None:
1338                return (10**9, int(section.order), section.id)
1339            return (int(desired), int(section.order), section.id)
1340
1341        sections_sorted = sorted(sections, key=sort_key)
1342        expected = 1
1343        changed: List[Section] = []
1344        for section in sections_sorted:
1345            if section.order != expected:
1346                section.order = expected
1347                changed.append(section)
1348            expected += 1
1349
1350        if changed:
1351            Section.objects.bulk_update(changed, ["order"])
1352
1353        return list(Section.objects.filter(portfolio=portfolio).order_by("order", "id"))
1354
1355    @transaction.atomic
1356    def bulk_create(self, *, portfolio_id: int, user, items: Sequence[Dict[str, Any]]) -> List[Section]:
1357        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, user=user)
1358        created: List[Section] = []
1359
1360        for item in items:
1361            self._reject_unknown_fields(item)
1362            created.append(self.create(portfolio_id=portfolio.id, user=user, validated_data=dict(item)))
1363
1364        return created
1365
1366    @transaction.atomic
1367    def bulk_update(self, *, portfolio_id: int, user, items: Sequence[Dict[str, Any]]) -> List[Section]:
1368        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, user=user)
1369        updated: List[Section] = []
1370
1371        for item in items:
1372            if not isinstance(item, dict):
1373                raise ValidationError({"detail": "Invalid bulk update payload."})
1374            section_id = item.get("id")
1375            if section_id is None:
1376                raise ValidationError({"id": "This field is required."})
1377            instance = self._resolve_section(portfolio_id=portfolio.id, section_id=int(section_id), user=user)
1378            payload = dict(item)
1379            payload.pop("id", None)
1380            self._reject_unknown_fields(payload)
1381            updated.append(self.update(instance=instance, user=user, validated_data=payload))
1382
1383        return updated

Service managing portfolio sections and their ordering.

allowed_fields = {'order', 'is_visible', 'name', 'config'}
not_found_message = 'Section not found.'
create_error_message = 'Could not create section.'
update_error_message = 'Could not update section.'
def list(self, *, portfolio_id: int, user) -> django.db.models.query.QuerySet: 
1173    def list(self, *, portfolio_id: int, user) -> QuerySet[Section]:
1174        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, user=user)
1175        return Section.objects.filter(portfolio=portfolio).order_by("order", "id")
def retrieve( self, *, portfolio_id: int, section_id: int, user) -> portfolio.models.Section: 
1177    def retrieve(self, *, portfolio_id: int, section_id: int, user) -> Section:
1178        return self._resolve_section(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, section_id=section_id, user=user)
@transaction.atomic
def create( self, *, portfolio_id: int, user, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> portfolio.models.Section: 
1180    @transaction.atomic
1181    def create(self, *, portfolio_id: int, user, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Section:
1182        self._reject_unknown_fields(validated_data)
1183        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, user=user)
1184
1185        payload = dict(validated_data)
1186
1187        if payload.get("name", None) is None:
1188            raise ValidationError({"name": "This field is required."})
1189
1190        payload["name"] = self._normalize_name(payload.get("name"))
1191        if "config" in payload:
1192            payload["config"] = self._normalize_config(payload.get("config"))
1193
1194        sections = self._lock_sections(portfolio=portfolio)
1195        self._resequence_orders(sections=sections)
1196
1197        desired_order = payload.get("order")
1198        if desired_order is None:
1199            desired_order = (sections[-1].order + 1) if sections else 1
1200        else:
1201            desired_order = self._normalize_order(desired_order)
1202
1203        max_allowed = len(sections) + 1
1204        desired_order = max(1, min(int(desired_order), max_allowed))
1205        payload["order"] = desired_order
1206
1207        # Insert behavior: shift existing sections down.
1208        shifted: List[Section] = []
1209        for section in sections:
1210            if section.order >= desired_order:
1211                section.order += 1
1212                shifted.append(section)
1213        if shifted:
1214            Section.objects.bulk_update(shifted, ["order"])
1215
1216        instance = Section(portfolio=portfolio, **payload)
1217
1218        try:
1219            instance.full_clean(exclude=None)
1220        except DjangoValidationError as exc:
1221            raise ValidationError(exc.message_dict or {"detail": exc.messages})
1222
1223        try:
1224            instance.save()
1225        except IntegrityError:
1226            raise ValidationError({"detail": self.create_error_message})
1227
1228        return instance
@transaction.atomic
def update( self, *, instance: portfolio.models.Section, user, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> portfolio.models.Section: 
1230    @transaction.atomic
1231    def update(self, *, instance: Section, user, validated_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Section:
1232        self._reject_unknown_fields(validated_data)
1233        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=instance.portfolio_id, user=user)
1234
1235        sections = self._lock_sections(portfolio=portfolio)
1236        self._resequence_orders(sections=sections)
1237
1238        locked_instance = next((s for s in sections if s.id == instance.id), None)
1239        if locked_instance is None:
1240            raise NotFound(self.not_found_message)
1241
1242        if "name" in validated_data:
1243            locked_instance.name = self._normalize_name(validated_data.get("name"))
1244        if "config" in validated_data:
1245            locked_instance.config = self._normalize_config(validated_data.get("config"))
1246        if "is_visible" in validated_data:
1247            locked_instance.is_visible = validated_data.get("is_visible")
1248
1249        if "order" in validated_data:
1250            desired_order = self._normalize_order(validated_data.get("order"))
1251            desired_order = max(1, min(int(desired_order), len(sections)))
1252            current_order = int(locked_instance.order)
1253
1254            if desired_order != current_order:
1255                affected: List[Section] = []
1256                if desired_order < current_order:
1257                    for section in sections:
1258                        if section.id == locked_instance.id:
1259                            continue
1260                        if desired_order <= int(section.order) < current_order:
1261                            section.order = int(section.order) + 1
1262                            affected.append(section)
1263                else:
1264                    for section in sections:
1265                        if section.id == locked_instance.id:
1266                            continue
1267                        if current_order < int(section.order) <= desired_order:
1268                            section.order = int(section.order) - 1
1269                            affected.append(section)
1270
1271                if affected:
1272                    Section.objects.bulk_update(affected, ["order"])
1273                locked_instance.order = desired_order
1274
1275        try:
1276            locked_instance.full_clean(exclude=None)
1277            locked_instance.save()
1278        except DjangoValidationError as exc:
1279            raise ValidationError(exc.message_dict or {"detail": exc.messages})
1280        except IntegrityError:
1281            raise ValidationError({"detail": self.update_error_message})
1282
1283        return locked_instance
@transaction.atomic
def delete(self, *, instance: portfolio.models.Section, user) -> None: 
1285    @transaction.atomic
1286    def delete(self, *, instance: Section, user) -> None:
1287        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=instance.portfolio_id, user=user)
1288
1289        sections = self._lock_sections(portfolio=portfolio)
1290        self._resequence_orders(sections=sections)
1291
1292        locked_instance = next((s for s in sections if s.id == instance.id), None)
1293        if locked_instance is None:
1294            raise NotFound(self.not_found_message)
1295
1296        deleted_order = int(locked_instance.order)
1297        locked_instance.delete()
1298
1299        affected: List[Section] = []
1300        for section in sections:
1301            if section.id == locked_instance.id:
1302                continue
1303            if int(section.order) > deleted_order:
1304                section.order = int(section.order) - 1
1305                affected.append(section)
1306
1307        if affected:
1308            Section.objects.bulk_update(affected, ["order"])
1309
1310        self._maybe_unpublish_if_invalid(portfolio=portfolio)
@transaction.atomic
def reorder( self, *, portfolio_id: int, user, order_pairs: Sequence[tuple[int, int]]) -> List[portfolio.models.Section]: 
1312    @transaction.atomic
1313    def reorder(self, *, portfolio_id: int, user, order_pairs: Sequence[tuple[int, int]]) -> List[Section]:
1314        """Reorder sections using (section_id, desired_order) pairs.
1315
1316        Applies a stable sort and then resequences to contiguous 1..n.
1317        """
1318
1319        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, user=user)
1320        sections = self._lock_sections(portfolio=portfolio)
1321        self._resequence_orders(sections=sections)
1322
1323        desired_map: Dict[int, int] = {}
1324        for section_id, desired_order in order_pairs:
1325            try:
1326                desired_map[int(section_id)] = self._normalize_order(desired_order)
1327            except (TypeError, ValueError):
1328                raise ValidationError({"order": "Invalid reorder payload."})
1329
1330        ids = {s.id for s in sections}
1331        unknown = sorted([sid for sid in desired_map.keys() if sid not in ids])
1332        if unknown:
1333            raise ValidationError({"detail": "One or more sections do not exist."})
1334
1335        def sort_key(section: Section) -> tuple:
1336            desired = desired_map.get(section.id)
1337            if desired is None:
1338                return (10**9, int(section.order), section.id)
1339            return (int(desired), int(section.order), section.id)
1340
1341        sections_sorted = sorted(sections, key=sort_key)
1342        expected = 1
1343        changed: List[Section] = []
1344        for section in sections_sorted:
1345            if section.order != expected:
1346                section.order = expected
1347                changed.append(section)
1348            expected += 1
1349
1350        if changed:
1351            Section.objects.bulk_update(changed, ["order"])
1352
1353        return list(Section.objects.filter(portfolio=portfolio).order_by("order", "id"))

Reorder sections using (section_id, desired_order) pairs.

Applies a stable sort and then resequences to contiguous 1..n.

@transaction.atomic
def bulk_create( self, *, portfolio_id: int, user, items: Sequence[Dict[str, Any]]) -> List[portfolio.models.Section]: 
1355    @transaction.atomic
1356    def bulk_create(self, *, portfolio_id: int, user, items: Sequence[Dict[str, Any]]) -> List[Section]:
1357        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, user=user)
1358        created: List[Section] = []
1359
1360        for item in items:
1361            self._reject_unknown_fields(item)
1362            created.append(self.create(portfolio_id=portfolio.id, user=user, validated_data=dict(item)))
1363
1364        return created
@transaction.atomic
def bulk_update( self, *, portfolio_id: int, user, items: Sequence[Dict[str, Any]]) -> List[portfolio.models.Section]: 
1366    @transaction.atomic
1367    def bulk_update(self, *, portfolio_id: int, user, items: Sequence[Dict[str, Any]]) -> List[Section]:
1368        portfolio = self._resolve_portfolio(portfolio_id=portfolio_id, user=user)
1369        updated: List[Section] = []
1370
1371        for item in items:
1372            if not isinstance(item, dict):
1373                raise ValidationError({"detail": "Invalid bulk update payload."})
1374            section_id = item.get("id")
1375            if section_id is None:
1376                raise ValidationError({"id": "This field is required."})
1377            instance = self._resolve_section(portfolio_id=portfolio.id, section_id=int(section_id), user=user)
1378            payload = dict(item)
1379            payload.pop("id", None)
1380            self._reject_unknown_fields(payload)
1381            updated.append(self.update(instance=instance, user=user, validated_data=payload))
1382
1383        return updated
class BlockService: 
1386class BlockService:
1387    """Service managing blocks within a section."""
1388
1389    allowed_fields = {"type", "order", "is_visible", "config"}
1390    not_found_message = "Block not found."
1391    create_error_message = "Could not create block."
1392    update_error_message = "Could not update block."
1393
1394    def _normalize_type(self, value: Any) -> str:
1395        t = "" if value is None else str(value)
1396        t = t.strip().upper()
1397        if not t:
1398            raise ValidationError({"type": "Type cannot be empty."})
1399
1400        allowed = {c for c, _ in Block.BlockType.choices}
1401        if t not in allowed:
1402            raise ValidationError({"type": "Invalid block type."})
1403        return t
1404
1405    def _normalize_order(self, value: Any) -> int:
1406        try:
1407            order = int(value)
1408        except (TypeError, ValueError):
1409            raise ValidationError({"order": "Order must be an integer."})
1410
1411        if order < 1:
1412            raise ValidationError({"order": "Order must be >= 1."})
1413        return order
1414
1415    def _normalize_config(self, value: Any) -> Dict[str, Any]:
1416        if value is None:
1417            raise ValidationError({"config": "Config must be an object."})
1418        if not isinstance(value, dict):
1419            raise ValidationError({"config": "Config must be an object."})
1420        return value
1421
1422    def _reject_unknown_fields(self, validated_data: Any) -> None:
1423        keys = set(getattr(validated_data, "keys", lambda: [])())
1424        unknown = keys - self.allowed_fields
1425        if unknown:
1426            raise ValidationError({key: "Unknown field." for key in sorted(unknown)})
1427
1428    def _resolve_section(self, *, portfolio_id: int, section_id: int, user, for_update: bool = False) -> Section:
1429        qs = Section.objects.select_related("portfolio")
1430        if for_update:
1431            qs = qs.select_for_update()
1432        try:
1433            return qs.get(id=section_id, portfolio_id=portfolio_id, portfolio__user_id=user.id)
1434        except Section.DoesNotExist:
1435            raise NotFound("Section not found.")
1436
1437    def _resolve_block(self, *, portfolio_id: int, section_id: int, block_id: int, user, for_update: bool = False) -> Block:
1438        qs = Block.objects.select_related("section", "section__portfolio")
1439        if for_update:
1440            qs = qs.select_for_update()
1441        try:
1442            return qs.get(id=block_id, section_id=section_id, section__portfolio_id=portfolio_id, section__portfolio__user_id=user.id)
1443        except Block.DoesNotExist:
1444            raise NotFound(self.not_found_message)
1445
1446    def _lock_blocks(self, *, section: Section) -> List[Block]:
1447        return list(
1448            Block.objects.select_for_update()
1449            .filter(section=section)
1450            .order_by("order", "id")
1451        )
1452
1453    def _resequence_orders(self, *, blocks: List[Block]) -> None:
1454        changed: List[Block] = []
1455        expected = 1
1456        for b in blocks:
